text: In Deutschland dürfen Menschen offen etwas gegen die Regierung sagen, weil …

en.text: In Germany, people are allowed to speak openly against the government because...

options.c: people have the right to vote.

en.context: Freedom of expression is a fundamental right in Germany, allowing you to share your opinions and criticize the government without fear. It is a cornerstone of a healthy democracy where every voice matters!

text: In Deutschland können Eltern bis zum 14. Lebensjahr ihres Kindes entscheiden, ob es in der Schule am …

en.text: In Germany, parents can decide until their child is 14 years old whether they will participate in ... at school.
en.context: In Germany, the state respects the parents' right to guide their children's religious upbringing until the age of 14. Interestingly, students can often choose an 'Ethics' class as an alternative to religious education!

text: Deutschland ist ein Rechtsstaat. Was ist damit gemeint?

options.a: Alle Einwohnerinnen/Einwohner und der Staat müssen sich an die Gesetze halten.
options.b: Der Staat muss sich nicht an die Gesetze halten.
options.c: Nur Deutsche müssen die Gesetze befolgen.

en.text: Germany is a state governed by the rule of law. What does this mean?

options.a: All residents and the state must abide by the laws.
options.b: The state does not have to abide by the laws.
options.c: Only Germans must follow the laws.

en.context: The 'rule of law' means that laws apply equally to every single person, including government officials and leaders. This ensures that justice is fair, predictable, and transparent for everyone!

text: Welches Recht gehört zu den Grundrechten in Deutschland?

en.text: Which right is among the basic rights in Germany?
en.context: Basic rights are essential human rights guaranteed by Germany's Basic Law to protect the dignity of every individual. These rights are so important that they are designed to be permanent and cannot be abolished!

text: Wahlen in Deutschland sind frei. Was bedeutet das?

options.a: Man darf Geld annehmen, wenn man dafür eine bestimmte Kandidatin/einen bestimmten Kandidaten wählt.
options.b: Nur Personen, die noch nie im Gefängnis waren, dürfen wählen.
options.c: Die Wählerin/der Wähler darf bei der Wahl weder beeinflusst noch zu einer bestimmten Stimmabgabe gezwungen werden und keine Nachteile durch die Wahl haben.

en.text: Elections in Germany are free. What does this mean?

options.a: You may accept money in exchange for voting for a specific candidate.
options.b: Only people who have never been in prison are allowed to vote.
options.c: The voter may neither be influenced nor forced to cast a specific vote and shall suffer no disadvantages as a result of the election.

en.context: Free elections mean that citizens can cast their votes in secret and without any pressure or coercion from others. This ensures that the results truly reflect the will of the people!

text: Wie heißt die deutsche Verfassung?

en.text: What is the name of the German constitution?
en.context: The German constitution is called the Basic Law (Grundgesetz). Interestingly, it was originally called a 'Basic Law' rather than a 'Constitution' because it was intended to be temporary until Germany was reunified!

text: Welches Recht gehört zu den Grundrechten, die nach der deutschen Verfassung garantiert werden? Das Recht auf …

en.text: Which right is among the basic rights guaranteed by the German constitution? The right to...
en.context: Freedom of faith and conscience ensures that everyone can practice their religion or beliefs freely. These basic rights are so important that they are protected from ever being abolished!

text: Was steht nicht im Grundgesetz von Deutschland?

options.a: Die Würde des Menschen ist unantastbar.
options.b: Alle sollen gleich viel Geld haben.
options.c: Jeder Mensch darf seine Meinung sagen.
options.d: Alle sind vor dem Gesetz gleich.

en.text: What is not written in the Basic Law of Germany?

options.b: Everyone should have the same amount of money.
options.c: Every person may express their opinion.
options.d: All are equal before the law.

en.context: The Basic Law guarantees equality of rights and opportunities, but it doesn't mandate equal wealth for everyone. Germany uses a 'social market economy' to balance free competition with strong social support.

text: Welches Grundrecht gilt in Deutschland nur für Ausländerinnen/Ausländer? Das Grundrecht auf …

en.text: Which basic right in Germany applies only to foreigners? The basic right to...
en.context: The right to asylum is specifically granted to foreigners who are persecuted in their home countries. This reflects Germany's commitment to protecting human rights and providing safety to those in need.

text: Was ist mit dem deutschen Grundgesetz vereinbar?

en.text: What is compatible with the German Basic Law?
en.context: While the Basic Law protects human dignity and forbids cruel punishment, legal penalties like monetary fines are allowed to uphold the law. A key fact is that the death penalty is strictly forbidden in Germany!

text: Wie wird die Verfassung der Bundesrepublik Deutschland genannt?

en.text: What is the constitution of the Federal Republic of Germany called?
en.context: Germany's constitution is called the Basic Law (Grundgesetz) to show it provides the essential framework for the state. Interestingly, it was originally intended as a provisional document, but it became so successful that it is now permanent!

text: Eine Partei im Deutschen Bundestag will die Pressefreiheit abschaffen. Ist das möglich?

options.a: Ja, wenn mehr als die Hälfte der Abgeordneten im Bundestag dafür sind.
options.b: Ja, aber dazu müssen zwei Drittel der Abgeordneten im Bundestag dafür sein.
options.c: Nein, denn die Pressefreiheit ist ein Grundrecht. Sie kann nicht abgeschafft werden.
options.d: Nein, denn nur der Bundesrat kann die Pressefreiheit abschaffen.

en.text: A party in the German Bundestag wants to abolish freedom of the press. Is this possible?

options.a: Yes, if more than half of the members of the Bundestag are in favor.
options.b: Yes, but two-thirds of the members of the Bundestag must be in favor.
options.c: No, because freedom of the press is a fundamental right. It cannot be abolished.
options.d: No, because only the Bundesrat can abolish freedom of the press.

en.context: Certain core values in the Basic Law are protected by an 'eternity clause,' meaning they can never be changed or abolished. This ensures that fundamental rights, like freedom of the press, are safe forever!

text: Im Parlament steht der Begriff "Opposition" für …

options.b: die Fraktion mit den meisten Abgeordneten.
options.c: alle Parteien, die bei der letzten Wahl die 5%-Hürde erreichen konnten.
options.d: alle Abgeordneten, die nicht zu der Regierungspartei/den Regierungsparteien gehören.

en.text: In parliament, the term "opposition" refers to...

options.b: the parliamentary group with the most members.
options.c: all parties that were able to meet the 5% threshold in the last election.
options.d: all members of parliament who do not belong to the governing party or parties.

en.context: The opposition is essential for a healthy democracy because they provide a critical check on the government's power. By offering alternative views, they help ensure that different perspectives are heard in parliament.

text: Meinungsfreiheit in Deutschland heißt, dass ich …

options.a: Passanten auf der Straße beschimpfen darf.
options.c: Nazi-, Hamas- oder Islamischer Staat-Symbole öffentlichtragen darf.
options.d: meine Meinung nur dann äußern darf, solange ich der Regierung nicht widerspreche.

en.text: Freedom of expression in Germany means that I...

options.a: may insult passers-by on the street.
options.b: can express my opinion on the internet.
options.c: may publicly wear Nazi, Hamas, or Islamic State symbols.
options.d: may only express my opinion as long as I do not contradict the government.

en.context: Freedom of expression allows you to share your opinions openly, whether in person or online. Just remember that this right comes with the responsibility to respect others and follow laws against hate speech!

text: Was verbietet das deutsche Grundgesetz?

en.text: What does the German Basic Law prohibit?
en.context: The Basic Law strictly prohibits forced labor to guarantee that every individual's freedom and dignity are respected. This strong protection is a key part of Germany's commitment to human rights for all.

text: Wann ist die Meinungsfreiheit in Deutschland eingeschränkt?

options.a: bei der öffentlichen Verbreitung falscher Behauptungen über einzelne Personen

en.text: When is freedom of expression restricted in Germany?

options.a: when publicly spreading false claims about individuals
options.b: when expressing opinions about the federal government

en.context: Freedom of speech is a vital right, but it ends where the rights of others begin, which is why lying about someone publicly is not allowed. This balance protects the personal honor and reputation of every individual!

text: Die deutschen Gesetze verbieten …

options.d: Ungleichbehandlung der Bürgerinnen und Bürger durch den Staat.

en.text: German laws prohibit...

options.d: unequal treatment of citizens by the state.

en.context: The German state is committed to equality, meaning no one can be discriminated against by the government. This ensures that every person, regardless of their background, has the same legal standing!

text: Welches Grundrecht ist in Artikel 1 des Grundgesetzes der Bundesrepublik Deutschland garantiert?

en.text: Which fundamental right is guaranteed in Article 1 of the Basic Law of the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: Article 1 is the heart of the German Basic Law, declaring that human dignity is untouchable and must be protected. A fascinating fact is that this article is 'eternal,' meaning it can never be changed or removed!

text: Was versteht man unter dem Recht der "Freizügigkeit" in Deutschland?

options.a: Man darf sich seinen Wohnort selbst aussuchen.
options.c: Man darf sich für eine andere Religion entscheiden.
options.d: Man darf sich in der Öffentlichkeit nur leicht bekleidet bewegen.

en.text: What is meant by the right to "freedom of movement" in Germany?

options.a: One may choose their own place of residence.
options.c: One may choose a different religion.
options.d: One may move in public only while lightly dressed.

en.context: Freedom of movement means you have the legal right to live and travel anywhere within Germany. This gives you the wonderful flexibility to find the city or town that feels most like home!

text: Eine Partei in Deutschland verfolgt das Ziel, eine Diktatur zu errichten. Sie ist dann …

en.text: A party in Germany aims to establish a dictatorship. In that case, it is...

options.b: oriented toward the rule of law.

en.context: Germany is a 'fortified democracy,' which means it can take action against groups that try to overthrow the democratic order. This safeguard ensures that the freedom and rights of all citizens remain protected!

text: Welches ist das Wappen der Bundesrepublik Deutschland?

en.text: Which is the coat of arms of the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: The Federal Eagle (Bundesadler) is the official national symbol of Germany, representing strength and sovereignty. Interestingly, the eagle has been used as a symbol in German-speaking lands for centuries!

text: Was für eine Staatsform hat Deutschland?

en.text: What is Germany's form of government?
en.context: Germany is a republic, which means it is not ruled by a monarch and power is held by elected representatives. This system ensures that the government is accountable to the people through democratic elections!

text: In Deutschland sind die meisten Erwerbstätigen …

options.c: selbstständig mit einer eigenen Firma tätig.
options.d: bei einer Firma oder Behörde beschäftigt.

en.text: In Germany, most employed people are...

options.d: employed by a company or a government agency.

en.context: The vast majority of workers in Germany are employed by private companies or government agencies. Much of Germany's economic strength comes from the 'Mittelstand'—the successful small and medium-sized family businesses!

text: Wie viele Bundesländer hat die Bundesrepublik Deutschland?

en.text: How many federal states does the Federal Republic of Germany have?
en.context: Germany is made up of 16 federal states, which allows different regions to have a say in their own education and culture. This federal system helps prevent too much power from being concentrated in one place.

text: Was ist kein Bundesland der Bundesrepublik Deutschland?

en.text: Which of these is not a federal state of the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: Alsace-Lorraine is a region that is now part of France, not Germany. A great way to learn the 16 actual states is to explore the diverse traditions, from the coasts of the north to the Alps in the south!

text: Deutschland ist …

en.text: Germany is...

options.b: a democratic and social federal state.
options.d: a social and socialist federal state.

en.context: Germany is built on the principle of protecting both individual freedom and social well-being. This means the government works to ensure no one is left behind through a strong social safety net!

text: Deutschland ist …

en.text: Germany is...
en.context: Being a federal state means power is shared between the central government and 16 individual states. This allows different regions to make local decisions, especially regarding education and culture!

text: Wer wählt in Deutschland die Abgeordneten zum Bundestag?

en.text: Who elects the members of the Bundestag in Germany?
en.context: In a democracy, power belongs to the people, who choose their representatives through free elections. Did you know that most German voters get two separate votes on their ballot?

text: Welches Tier ist das Wappentier der Bundesrepublik Deutschland?

en.text: Which animal is on the coat of arms of the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: The Federal Eagle (Bundesadler) is the proud national symbol of Germany, representing strength and sovereignty. You can see it on official government documents and buildings across the country!

text: Was ist kein Merkmal unserer Demokratie?

en.text: Which of these is not a characteristic of our democracy?
en.context: Freedom of the press is a cornerstone of democracy, ensuring that journalists can report the truth without government interference. A free press acts as a watchdog to keep the government accountable!

text: Die Zusammenarbeit von Parteien zur Bildung einer Regierung nennt man in Deutschland …

en.text: The cooperation of parties to form a government in Germany is called...
en.context: Since it is rare for one party to win an absolute majority, parties team up in a coalition to govern together. A fun detail is that they write a detailed 'coalition agreement' to decide their shared goals!

text: Was ist keine staatliche Gewalt in Deutschland?

en.text: Which of the following is not a branch of government in Germany?
en.context: The press is an independent observer, not a government branch like the legislature or courts. It is often called the 'fourth estate' because it helps keep the government honest!

text: Welche Aussage ist richtig? In Deutschland …

options.a: sind Staat und Religionsgemeinschaften voneinander getrennt.
options.c: ist der Staat abhängig von den Religionsgemeinschaften.
options.d: bilden Staat und Religionsgemeinschaften eine Einheit.

en.text: Which statement is correct? In Germany...

options.a: state and religious communities are separate.
options.c: the state is dependent on religious communities.
options.d: state and religious communities form a single unit.

en.context: Germany maintains a separation between the state and religious communities to ensure fairness and neutrality. This protects your freedom to practice any religion—or none at all!

text: Was ist Deutschland nicht?

en.text: What is Germany not?

options.b: a state under the rule of law

en.context: Germany is a republic, which means it is governed by elected representatives rather than a king or queen. It's a great system designed to ensure power is shared and balanced!

text: Womit finanziert der deutsche Staat die Sozialversicherung?

en.text: How does the German state finance social security?
en.context: Social security is funded by contributions shared between employees and employers. This system is built on 'solidarity,' ensuring everyone is supported during tough times or retirement!

text: Welche Maßnahme schafft in Deutschland soziale Sicherheit?

en.text: Which measure provides social security in Germany?
en.context: Health insurance is a key part of Germany's social security system, ensuring everyone has access to medical care. Fun fact: Germany's social security system is one of the oldest in the world, originally established by Otto von Bismarck!

text: Wie werden die Regierungschefinnen/Regierungschefs der meisten Bundesländer in Deutschland genannt?

en.text: What are the heads of government of most German federal states called?
en.context: The Minister-President leads the government of a federal state, managing regional affairs. While the Chancellor leads the whole country, the Minister-President focuses on state-specific tasks like education!

text: Die Bundesrepublik Deutschland ist ein demokratischer und sozialer …

en.text: The Federal Republic of Germany is a democratic and social...
en.context: Germany is a federal state, meaning power is shared between the central government and 16 individual states. This system ensures that different regions have a strong voice in how the country is governed.

text: Was hat jedes deutsche Bundesland?

en.text: What does every German federal state have?
en.context: Every federal state has its own government to handle local laws and administration. This is why you'll find that things like school holidays vary from one state to another!

text: Mit welchen Worten beginnt die deutsche Nationalhymne?

en.text: What are the opening words of the German national anthem?
en.context: The opening words 'Unity and Justice and Freedom' reflect the fundamental values of the German people. Interestingly, only the third stanza of the national anthem is officially used today!

text: Warum gibt es in einer Demokratie mehr als eine Partei?

options.a: weil dadurch die unterschiedlichen Meinungen der Bürgerinnen und Bürger vertreten werden
options.b: damit Bestechung in der Politik begrenzt wird

en.text: Why are there more than one party in a democracy?

options.a: because the different opinions of citizens are represented

en.context: Having multiple parties ensures that a wide variety of political opinions and interests are represented in government. This diversity is key to a healthy democracy where compromise leads to better laws!

text: Wer beschließt in Deutschland ein neues Gesetz?

en.text: Who passes a new law in Germany?
en.context: The parliament, primarily the Bundestag, is where elected representatives discuss and vote on new laws for the country. A cool fact: the Bundestag is one of the largest democratic parliaments in the world!

text: Wann kann in Deutschland eine Partei verboten werden?

options.a: wenn ihr Wahlkampf zu teuer ist
options.b: wenn sie gegen die Verfassung kämpft
options.c: wenn sie Kritik am Staatsoberhaupt äußert
options.d: wenn ihr Programm eine neue Richtung vorschlägt

en.text: When can a party be banned in Germany?

options.a: if its election campaign is too expensive
options.b: if it fights against the constitution
options.c: if it expresses criticism of the head of state
options.d: if its program proposes a new direction

en.context: Germany practices 'fortified democracy,' meaning parties can be banned if they actively seek to overthrow the democratic order. This safeguard helps protect the freedom and rights of all citizens.

text: Wen kann man als Bürgerin/Bürger in Deutschland nicht direkt wählen?

en.text: Whom can citizens in Germany not vote for directly?
en.context: The Federal President is elected by a special assembly called the Federal Convention rather than directly by the people because the role is mainly representative. This keeps the head of state above the daily political fray!

text: Zu welcher Versicherung gehört die Pflegeversicherung?

en.text: Which type of insurance does long-term care insurance belong to?
en.context: Long-term care insurance is one of the five pillars of the German social security system, ensuring everyone gets support in old age. It was introduced in 1995 to provide a reliable safety net for those needing constant care.

text: Der deutsche Staat hat viele Aufgaben. Welche Aufgabe gehört dazu?

options.c: Er versorgt alle Einwohnerinnen und Einwohner kostenlos mit Zeitungen.

en.text: The German state has many responsibilities. Which of these is one of them?

options.c: It provides all residents with free newspapers.

en.context: The state provides essential public infrastructure like roads and schools to ensure a high quality of life for all. Fun fact: Germany is world-famous for its Autobahn network!

text: Der deutsche Staat hat viele Aufgaben. Welche Aufgabe gehört nicht dazu?

options.a: Er bezahlt für alle Staatsangehörigen Urlaubsreisen.

en.text: The German state has many responsibilities. Which of these is NOT one of them?

options.a: It pays for vacations for all citizens.

en.context: While the state provides a strong social safety net, personal leisure and vacations are a private responsibility. Interestingly, 'Urlaub' (vacation) is a highly valued part of German work-life balance!

text: Welches Organ gehört nicht zu den Verfassungsorganen Deutschlands?

en.text: Which body is not one of Germany's constitutional bodies?
en.context: Germany's core constitutional bodies include the Bundestag, Bundesrat, and Federal President, but there is no permanent national citizens' assembly. You can still influence politics by voting and joining local community groups!

text: Wer bestimmt in Deutschland die Schulpolitik?

en.text: Who determines school policy in Germany?
en.context: Education is managed by the 16 federal states (Bundesländer) rather than the central government, allowing for regional preferences. This means school holidays can differ depending on which state you live in!

text: Die Wirtschaftsform in Deutschland nennt man …

en.text: The economic system in Germany is called...
en.context: The social market economy combines free-market competition with social protections to ensure fairness and stability. It is a 'middle way' designed to balance economic growth with social justice!

text: Zu einem demokratischen Rechtsstaat gehört es nicht, dass …

options.a: Menschen sich kritisch über die Regierung äußern können.
options.c: Menschen von einer Privatpolizei ohne Grund verhaftet werden.
options.d: jemand ein Verbrechen begeht und deshalb verhaftet wird.

en.text: Which of the following is NOT a feature of a democratic state based on the rule of law?

options.a: People being able to express criticism of the government.
options.b: Citizens being allowed to demonstrate peacefully.
options.c: People being arrested by a private police force without cause.
options.d: Someone committing a crime and being arrested because of it.

en.context: In a state based on the rule of law, only authorized state organs can arrest individuals, and they must follow strict legal procedures. This prevents arbitrary power and protects your fundamental rights! Remember, the law applies equally to everyone.

text: Was bedeutet "Volkssouveränität"? Alle Staatsgewalt geht vom ...

en.text: What does "popular sovereignty" mean? All state authority emanates from the...
en.context: Popular sovereignty means that the ultimate power in a democracy rests with the citizens, who exercise it through elections. It ensures that the government represents the will of the people! In Germany, this is most clearly seen during the federal elections.

text: Was bedeutet "Rechtsstaat" in Deutschland?

options.c: Die Bürgerinnen und Bürger entscheiden über Gesetze.
options.d: Der Staat muss die Gesetze einhalten.

en.text: What does "Rechtsstaat" (state based on the rule of law) mean in Germany?

options.d: The state must comply with the law.

en.context: A 'Rechtsstaat' ensures that the government itself is bound by the law and cannot act randomly or unfairly. This provides safety and predictability for everyone living in Germany. Did you know you can actually challenge state decisions in an administrative court?

text: Was ist keine staatliche Gewalt in Deutschland?

en.text: Which of the following is NOT a branch of government in Germany?
en.context: Germany uses a system of 'checks and balances' divided into the Legislative, Executive, and Judicial branches to prevent any one group from having total power. 'Directive' is not a branch of government. This division is key to maintaining a stable democracy!

text: Was zeigt dieses Bild?

en.text: What does this image show?

options.a: the seat of the Bundestag in Berlin
options.b: the Federal Constitutional Court in Karlsruhe

en.context: The Reichstag building in Berlin is the seat of the Bundestag, where Germany's elected representatives meet to pass laws. Its famous glass dome symbolizes transparency in government. Interestingly, the dome allows citizens to literally look down upon the politicians at work!

text: Welches Amt gehört in Deutschland zur Gemeindeverwaltung?

en.text: Which office is part of the local municipal administration in Germany?
en.context: The public order office (Ordnungsamt) handles local matters like public safety and permits to keep your community running smoothly. It is often the most versatile office in a city's administration!

text: Wer wird meistens zur Präsidentin/zum Präsidenten des Deutschen Bundestages gewählt?

options.b: die Ministerpräsidentin/der Ministerpräsident des größten Bundeslandes
options.d: eine Abgeordnete/ein Abgeordneter der stärksten Fraktion

en.text: Who is usually elected as the President of the German Bundestag?

options.b: the Minister-President of the largest federal state
options.d: a member of the largest parliamentary group

en.context: The President of the Bundestag is usually chosen from the largest parliamentary group to ensure the parliament is managed effectively. This person acts as a neutral moderator during intense political debates!

text: Wer ernennt in Deutschland die Ministerinnen/die Minister der Bundesregierung?

en.text: Who appoints the ministers of the federal government in Germany?

options.a: the President of the Federal Constitutional Court

en.context: While the Chancellor proposes the ministers, the Federal President officially appoints them to maintain a formal balance of power. The Federal President serves as the symbolic head of state and represents Germany globally.

text: Vor wie vielen Jahren gab es erstmals eine jüdische Gemeinde auf dem Gebiet des heutigen Deutschlands?

en.text: How many years ago was there first a Jewish community in the territory of present-day Germany?
en.context: Jewish communities have lived in German lands since the Roman era, dating back about 1,700 years. This long history shows that Jewish culture has been an integral part of the region's identity for centuries.

text: In Deutschland gehören der Bundestag und der Bundesrat zur …

en.text: In Germany, the Bundestag and the Bundesrat are part of the...
en.context: The Bundestag and Bundesrat make up the legislative branch, which is responsible for debating and passing the laws we all follow. A helpful tip: 'Legislative' comes from the Latin word 'lex,' which simply means law!

text: Was bedeutet "Volkssouveränität"?

options.a: Die Königin/der König herrscht über das Volk.
options.b: Das Bundesverfassungsgericht steht über der Verfassung.
options.c: Die Interessenverbände üben die Souveränität zusammen mit der Regierung aus.
options.d: Die Staatsgewalt geht vom Volke aus.

en.text: What does 'popular sovereignty' mean?

options.a: The Queen/King rules over the people.
options.b: The Federal Constitutional Court stands above the constitution.
options.c: Interest groups exercise sovereignty together with the government.
options.d: State power derives from the people.

en.context: In a democracy, the people are the ultimate source of power, which is why your vote matters so much! This principle ensures that the government serves the citizens and not the other way around.

text: Wenn das Parlament eines deutschen Bundeslandes gewählt wird, nennt man das …

en.text: When the parliament of a German federal state is elected, it is called a...
en.context: Germany is a federal republic with 16 states, and each has its own parliament chosen through state elections. It's a great way to ensure that regional needs are represented in government!

text: Was gehört in Deutschland nicht zur Exekutive?

en.text: What is not part of the executive branch in Germany?
en.context: The courts belong to the judicial branch, which acts as an independent referee to make sure laws are applied fairly. This separation of powers is key to protecting your individual rights!

text: Die Bundesrepublik Deutschland ist heute gegliedert in …

en.text: The Federal Republic of Germany is currently divided into...

options.b: one eastern state and one western state.

en.context: Germany organizes its administration into three levels—the federation, states, and municipalities—to balance national goals with local needs. This means many daily services are managed right in your own neighborhood!

text: Es gehört nicht zu den Aufgaben des Deutschen Bundestages, …

en.text: It is not a task of the German Bundestag to...
en.context: The Bundestag's main role is to pass laws and oversee the government; the Chancellor is actually the one who selects the ministers for the cabinet. Think of the Bundestag as the law-makers and the cabinet as the law-executors!

text: Welche Städte haben die größten jüdischen Gemeinden in Deutschland?

en.text: Which cities have the largest Jewish communities in Germany?
en.context: Berlin and Munich are Germany's largest cities and host the most vibrant and numerous Jewish communities today. It's wonderful to see these communities thriving and contributing to the city's cultural diversity!

text: Was ist in Deutschland vor allem eine Aufgabe der Bundesländer?

en.text: What is primarily a responsibility of the federal states in Germany?
en.context: Germany is a federal republic, meaning power is split between the central government and the 16 states, with education falling under state control. This is why school holidays can be different depending on which state you live in!

text: Warum kontrolliert der Staat in Deutschland das Schulwesen?

options.a: weil es in Deutschland nur staatliche Schulen gibt
options.b: weil alle Schülerinnen und Schüler einen Schulabschluss haben müssen
options.c: weil es in den Bundesländern verschiedene Schulen gibt
options.d: weil es nach dem Grundgesetz seine Aufgabe ist

en.text: Why does the state control the education system in Germany?

options.a: because there are only state schools in Germany
options.b: because all students must obtain a school-leaving certificate
options.c: because there are various types of schools in the federal states
options.d: because it is its duty according to the Basic Law

en.context: The Basic Law assigns education to the states to ensure that no single central authority has total control over learning. This principle, known as 'Kulturhoheit,' helps preserve regional identities and diversity across the country.

text: Die Bundesrepublik Deutschland hat einen dreistufigen Verwaltungsaufbau. Wie heißt die unterste politische Stufe?

en.text: The Federal Republic of Germany has a three-tier administrative structure. What is the lowest political level called?
en.context: Municipalities are the local level of government and handle the things that affect your daily life most, like parks and local roads. You'll interact with this level most often when visiting your local Bürgeramt (citizens' office)!

text: Der deutsche Bundespräsident Gustav Heinemann gibt Helmut Schmidt 1974 die Ernennungsurkunde zum deutschen Bundeskanzler. Was gehört zu den Aufgaben der deutschen Bundespräsidentin/des deutschen Bundespräsidenten?

options.d: Sie/Er schlägt die Kanzlerin/den Kanzler zur Wahl vor.

en.text: In 1974, German Federal President Gustav Heinemann presented Helmut Schmidt with the appointment certificate as German Federal Chancellor. What are the duties of the German Federal President?

options.a: He/She manages the affairs of government.
options.d: He/She proposes the Chancellor for election.

en.context: The Federal President serves as the head of state and performs important ceremonial duties, including proposing the Chancellor for election. While the Chancellor manages politics, the President acts as a unifying symbol for all Germans.

text: Wo hält sich die deutsche Bundeskanzlerin/der deutsche Bundeskanzler am häufigsten auf? Am häufigsten ist sie/er …

options.a: in Bonn, weil sich dort das Bundeskanzleramt und der Bundestag befinden.
options.b: auf Schloss Meseberg, dem Gästehaus der Bundesregierung, um Staatsgäste zu empfangen.
options.c: auf Schloss Bellevue, dem Amtssitz der Bundespräsidentin/des Bundespräsidenten, um Staatsgäste zu empfangen.
options.d: in Berlin, weil sich dort das Bundeskanzleramt und der Bundestag befinden.

en.text: Where is the German Federal Chancellor most often located? Most often, he/she is...

options.a: in Bonn, because the Federal Chancellery and the Bundestag are located there.
options.b: at Schloss Meseberg, the Federal Government's guest house, to receive state guests.
options.c: at Schloss Bellevue, the official residence of the Federal President, to receive state guests.
options.d: in Berlin, because the Federal Chancellery and the Bundestag are located there.

en.context: Schloss Meseberg serves as the official guest house for the Federal Government, providing a beautiful and welcoming setting for international leaders. It's a wonderful example of how Germany blends historic architecture with modern diplomacy!

text: Wie heißt die jetzige Bundeskanzlerin/der jetzige Bundeskanzler von Deutschland?

en.text: Who is the current Federal Chancellor of Germany?
en.context: The Federal Chancellor is the most powerful political figure in Germany, leading the government and setting the policy direction. This role is key to ensuring the country runs smoothly and effectively!

text: Die beiden größten Fraktionen im Deutschen Bundestag heißen zurzeit …

en.text: The two largest parliamentary groups in the German Bundestag are currently...
en.context: Parliamentary groups are formed by parties to coordinate their votes and influence lawmaking. Having a large group means more weight in debates and a bigger say in how the country is governed!

text: Wie heißt das Parlament für ganz Deutschland?

en.text: What is the name of the parliament for all of Germany?
en.context: The Bundestag is the national parliament where representatives elected by citizens meet to create laws for all of Germany. It is often described as the heart of German democracy!

text: Wie heißt Deutschlands heutiges Staatsoberhaupt?

en.text: Who is the current head of state of Germany?
en.context: The Federal President is the head of state and represents Germany both at home and abroad. While the Chancellor manages politics, the President serves as a unifying figure and a moral guide for the nation!

text: Was bedeutet die Abkürzung CDU in Deutschland?

en.text: What does the abbreviation CDU stand for in Germany?
en.context: The CDU is one of Germany's major center-right political parties. Fun fact: it has provided many of Germany's Chancellors since the country's foundation!

text: Was ist die Bundeswehr?

en.text: What is the Bundeswehr?
en.context: The Bundeswehr is the unified military force of Germany, including the army, navy, and air force. It is known as a 'parliamentary army' because the Bundestag must approve most of its overseas missions!

text: Was bedeutet die Abkürzung SPD?

en.text: What does the abbreviation SPD stand for?
en.context: The SPD is the oldest major political party in Germany, focusing on social justice and workers' rights. It played a key role in building the modern German welfare state that helps citizens today!

text: Was bedeutet die Abkürzung FDP in Deutschland?

en.text: What does the abbreviation FDP stand for in Germany?
en.context: The FDP is a liberal party that emphasizes individual freedom and free-market economics. Because of its size, the FDP often acts as a 'kingmaker' when deciding which parties will form a coalition government!

text: Welches Gericht in Deutschland ist zuständig für die Auslegung des Grundgesetzes?

en.text: Which court in Germany is responsible for the interpretation of the Basic Law?
en.context: The Federal Constitutional Court ensures that all laws and government actions align with the Basic Law. To keep it independent from the political bustle of Berlin, the court is located in the city of Karlsruhe!

text: Wer wählt die Bundeskanzlerin/den Bundeskanzler in Deutschland?

en.text: Who elects the Federal Chancellor in Germany?
en.context: The Bundestag, as the parliament elected by the people, chooses the Chancellor to ensure the government has democratic support. Fun fact: This election usually happens after parties negotiate to form a coalition government!

text: Wer leitet das deutsche Bundeskabinett?

en.text: Who leads the German Federal Cabinet?
en.context: The Federal Chancellor is the head of government and decides the general guidelines of Germany's political direction. Interestingly, the Chancellor is the most powerful political figure in the country!

text: Wer wählt die deutsche Bundeskanzlerin/den deutschen Bundeskanzler?

en.text: Who elects the German Federal Chancellor?
en.context: The Bundestag elects the Chancellor to maintain a direct link between the voters' will and the executive branch. Did you know that a majority of members must vote 'yes' for the election to be successful?

text: Welche Hauptaufgabe hat die deutsche Bundespräsidentin/der deutsche Bundespräsident? Sie/Er …

en.text: What is the main task of the German Federal President? He/she...
en.context: While the Chancellor handles politics, the President serves as the neutral head of state and represents Germany both at home and abroad. Their role is mostly ceremonial, focusing on unity and moral leadership!

text: Wer bildet den deutschen Bundesrat?

options.b: die Ministerinnen und Minister der Bundesregierung

en.text: Who makes up the German Bundesrat?

options.b: the ministers of the Federal Government
options.c: the government representatives of the federal states

en.context: The Bundesrat consists of members from the 16 federal states, ensuring that regional interests are considered when making national laws. It is a wonderful example of how Germany shares power between the center and the states!

text: Wer wählt in Deutschland die Bundespräsidentin/den Bundespräsidenten?

en.text: Who elects the Federal President in Germany?
en.context: The Federal Convention is a special assembly created just to elect the President. It’s a unique mix of parliament members and delegates from across the country!

text: Wer ist das Staatsoberhaupt der Bundesrepublik Deutschland?

en.text: Who is the head of state of the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: The Federal President serves as the official representative of Germany and a symbol of national unity. Did you know the President usually focuses more on ceremonial duties than daily politics?

text: Die parlamentarische Opposition im Deutschen Bundestag …

options.d: schlägt die Regierungschefinnen/Regierungschefs der Länder vor.

en.text: The parliamentary opposition in the German Bundestag...

options.b: decides who becomes a federal minister.
options.c: determines who sits in the Bundesrat.
options.d: proposes the heads of government of the federal states.

en.context: The opposition plays a vital role by questioning the government and suggesting better alternatives. This keeps the democratic process fair and transparent for everyone!

text: Wie nennt man in Deutschland die Vereinigung von Abgeordneten einer Partei im Parlament?

en.text: In Germany, what is the group of members of parliament from one party called?
en.context: Members of the same party form a 'parliamentary group' (Fraktion) to work together effectively. This teamwork helps them organize their goals and make their voices heard more clearly.

text: Die deutschen Bundesländer wirken an der Gesetzgebung des Bundes mit durch …

en.text: The German federal states participate in federal legislation through...
en.context: The Bundesrat allows the 16 federal states to voice their opinions on national laws. It's a great example of how Germany shares power between the center and the regions!

text: In Deutschland kann ein Regierungswechsel in einem Bundesland Auswirkungen auf die Bundespolitik haben. Das Regieren wird …

options.a: schwieriger, wenn sich dadurch die Mehrheit im Bundestag ändert.
options.b: leichter, wenn dadurch neue Parteien in den Bundesrat kommen.
options.c: schwieriger, wenn dadurch die Mehrheit im Bundesrat verändert wird.
options.d: leichter, wenn es sich um ein reiches Bundesland handelt.

en.text: In Germany, a change of government in a federal state can have an impact on federal politics. Governing becomes...

options.a: more difficult if the majority in the Bundestag changes as a result.
options.b: easier if new parties enter the Bundesrat as a result.
options.c: more difficult if the majority in the Bundesrat is altered as a result.
options.d: easier if it is a wealthy federal state.

en.context: The Bundesrat represents the 16 federal states, and since many laws need its approval, a shift in state power can make federal governing more challenging. Fun fact: The Bundesrat is a unique feature of German federalism, ensuring states have a direct say in national laws!

text: Was bedeutet die Abkürzung CSU in Deutschland?

en.text: What does the abbreviation CSU stand for in Germany?
en.context: The CSU stands for the Christian Social Union, a major political party that operates exclusively in the state of Bavaria. A cool detail: They are sister parties with the CDU, often working together to form a powerful coalition in the national government!

text: Je mehr "Zweitstimmen" eine Partei bei einer Bundestagswahl bekommt, desto …

options.b: mehr Direktkandidaten der Partei ziehen ins Parlament ein.
options.c: größer ist das Risiko, eine Koalition bilden zu müssen.
options.d: mehr Sitze erhält die Partei im Parlament.

en.text: The more 'second votes' a party receives in a Bundestag election, the...

options.a: fewer first votes it can have.
options.b: more direct candidates of the party enter parliament.
options.c: greater the risk of having to form a coalition.
options.d: more seats the party receives in parliament.

en.context: In the German electoral system, the second vote is the most important because it determines the proportional percentage of seats a party gets in parliament. Remember: Your second vote is effectively your vote for the party itself!

text: Ab welchem Alter darf man in Deutschland an der Wahl zum Deutschen Bundestag teilnehmen?

en.text: From what age are you allowed to vote in the German Bundestag election?
en.context: You are eligible to vote in federal elections once you turn 18, marking your transition into full political adulthood. Interestingly, some German states now allow citizens to vote in state or local elections as young as 16!

text: Was gilt für die meisten Kinder in Deutschland?

en.text: What applies to most children in Germany?
en.context: Compulsory education (Schulpflicht) ensures that every child has access to learning and equal opportunities for their future. It is a fundamental part of the German social system to ensure no child is left behind!

text: Wie kann jemand, der den Holocaust leugnet, bestraft werden?

options.d: mit Freiheitsstrafe bis zu fünf Jahren oder mit Geldstrafe

en.text: How can someone who denies the Holocaust be punished?

options.b: up to 100 hours of community service
options.c: not at all, Holocaust denial is permitted
options.d: with imprisonment for up to five years or a fine

en.context: Germany has strict laws against Holocaust denial to protect human dignity and prevent hate speech. This is part of Germany's 'defensive democracy,' which actively works to protect the free democratic order.

text: Was bezahlt man in Deutschland automatisch, wenn man fest angestellt ist?

en.text: What do you automatically pay in Germany if you are permanently employed?
en.context: These contributions automatically fund your healthcare, pension, and unemployment insurance for a safer future. It's a great system that ensures you're protected even during tough times!

text: Wenn Abgeordnete im Deutschen Bundestag ihre Fraktion wechseln, …

options.a: dürfen sie nicht mehr an den Sitzungen des Parlaments teilnehmen.
options.b: kann die Regierung ihre Mehrheit verlieren.
options.c: muss die Bundespräsidentin/der Bundespräsident zuvor ihr/sein Einverständnis geben.
options.d: dürfen die Wählerinnen/Wähler dieser Abgeordneten noch einmal wählen.

en.text: If members of the German Bundestag change their parliamentary group...

options.a: they are no longer allowed to participate in parliamentary sessions.
options.b: the government may lose its majority.
options.c: the Federal President must first give their consent.
options.d: the voters of these members are allowed to vote again.

en.context: Because the government needs a majority to pass laws, members switching groups can change the balance of power. This is why stability in coalition agreements is so important in German politics!

text: Wer bezahlt in Deutschland die Sozialversicherungen?

en.text: Who pays for social security in Germany?
en.context: Social security is funded through a partnership between you and your employer to ensure fair access for all. This principle of solidarity is a cornerstone of the German social state.

text: Was gehört nicht zur gesetzlichen Sozialversicherung?

en.text: Which of the following is not part of the statutory social security system?
en.context: While the state provides essential health and pension insurance, life insurance is a private contract you choose yourself. Germany's statutory system is designed to provide a basic safety net for every citizen.

text: Gewerkschaften sind Interessenverbände der …

en.text: Trade unions are interest groups for...
en.context: Trade unions protect employees' rights and negotiate for better wages and working conditions. Did you know that Germany's 'social partnership' between unions and employers helps keep the economy stable and fair for everyone?

text: Womit kann man in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland geehrt werden, wenn man auf politischem, wirtschaftlichem, kulturellem, geistigem oder sozialem Gebiet eine besondere Leistung erbracht hat? Mit dem …

en.text: In the Federal Republic of Germany, what can be awarded to someone who has achieved a special accomplishment in a political, economic, cultural, intellectual, or social field? The...

options.d: Honorary title "Hero of the German Democratic Republic"

en.context: The Federal Cross of Merit is Germany's highest tribute to citizens who make a positive difference in society. It's wonderful because it honors everyone from world-famous scientists to local community volunteers!

text: Was wird in Deutschland als "Ampelkoalition" bezeichnet? Die Zusammenarbeit …

options.a: der Bundestagsfraktionen von CDU und CSU
options.b: von SPD, FDP und Bündnis 90/Die Grünen in einer Regierung
options.c: von CSU, Die LINKE und Bündnis 90/Die Grünen in einer Regierung
options.d: der Bundestagsfraktionen von CDU und SPD

en.text: What is referred to as a "traffic light coalition" in Germany? The cooperation...

options.a: of the CDU and CSU parliamentary groups
options.b: of the SPD, FDP, and Alliance 90/The Greens in a government
options.c: of the CSU, The Left, and Alliance 90/The Greens in a government
options.d: of the CDU and SPD parliamentary groups

en.context: This name comes from the parties' official colors: Red (SPD), Yellow (FDP), and Green (Greens). In Germany, parties often team up in coalitions like this to build a strong, shared government!

text: Eine Frau in Deutschland verliert ihre Arbeit. Was darf nicht der Grund für diese Entlassung sein?

options.a: Die Frau ist lange krank und arbeitsunfähig.
options.b: Die Frau kam oft zu spät zur Arbeit.
options.c: Die Frau erledigt private Sachen während der Arbeitszeit.
options.d: Die Frau bekommt ein Kind und ihr Chef weiß das.

en.text: A woman in Germany loses her job. What may not be the reason for this dismissal?

options.a: The woman is on long-term sick leave and unable to work.
options.b: The woman was often late for work.
options.c: The woman attends to private matters during working hours.
options.d: The woman is having a child and her boss knows about it.

en.context: German law strongly protects parents and pregnant women to ensure they don't lose their livelihoods while starting a family. This is part of the 'Mutterschutz' (maternity protection) laws that support families!

text: Was ist eine Aufgabe von Wahlhelferinnen/Wahlhelfern in Deutschland?

options.a: Sie helfen alten Menschen bei der Stimmabgabe in der Wahlkabine.
options.b: Sie schreiben die Wahlbenachrichtigungen vor der Wahl.
options.c: Sie geben Zwischenergebnisse an die Medien weiter.
options.d: Sie zählen die Stimmen nach dem Ende der Wahl.

en.text: What is a task of election workers in Germany?

options.a: They help elderly people cast their votes in the voting booth.
options.b: They write the election notifications before the election.
options.c: They pass interim results to the media.
options.d: They count the votes after the election ends.

en.context: Election workers are volunteers who ensure every vote is counted accurately and fairly. It's a great way for ordinary citizens to participate directly in the heart of democracy!

text: In Deutschland helfen ehrenamtliche Wahlhelferinnen und Wahlhelfer bei den Wahlen. Was ist eine Aufgabe von Wahlhelferinnen/Wahlhelfern?

options.a: Sie helfen Kindern und alten Menschen beim Wählen.
options.b: Sie schreiben Karten und Briefe mit der Angabe des Wahllokals.
options.c: Sie geben Zwischenergebnisse an Journalisten weiter.
options.d: Sie zählen die Stimmen nach dem Ende der Wahl.

en.text: In Germany, volunteer election workers assist during elections. What is a task of election workers?

options.a: They help children and elderly people vote.
options.b: They write cards and letters specifying the polling station.
options.c: They pass interim results to journalists.
options.d: They count the votes after the election ends.

en.context: Volunteer election workers help maintain transparency by counting votes in public view after polls close. It's a wonderful way for citizens to get directly involved in their democracy!

text: Für wie viele Jahre wird der Bundestag in Deutschland gewählt?

en.text: For how many years is the Bundestag in Germany elected?
en.context: The Bundestag is elected for a four-year term to balance political stability with the need for regular democratic updates. You'll find that this cycle is a key rhythm of German political life!

text: Bei einer Bundestagswahl in Deutschland darf jede/jeder wählen, die/der …

options.a: in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland wohnt und wählen möchte.
options.b: Bürgerin/Bürger der Bundesrepublik Deutschland ist und mindestens 18 Jahre alt ist.
options.c: seit mindestens 3 Jahren in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland lebt.
options.d: Bürgerin/Bürger der Bundesrepublik Deutschland ist und mindestens 21 Jahre alt ist.

en.text: In a federal election in Germany, anyone may vote who...

options.a: lives in the Federal Republic of Germany and wishes to vote.
options.b: is a citizen of the Federal Republic of Germany and is at least 18 years old.
options.c: has lived in the Federal Republic of Germany for at least 3 years.
options.d: is a citizen of the Federal Republic of Germany and is at least 21 years old.

en.context: Eligibility for federal elections requires German citizenship and being at least 18 years old to ensure a legal and mature electorate. Interestingly, some local elections allow non-German EU citizens to vote too!

text: Wie oft gibt es normalerweise Bundestagswahlen in Deutschland?

en.text: How often are federal elections normally held in Germany?
en.context: Federal elections are normally held every four years to allow citizens to decide on the country's direction. This regular heartbeat of democracy ensures that the government always reflects the people's current will!

text: Für wie viele Jahre wird der Bundestag in Deutschland gewählt?

en.text: For how many years is the Bundestag in Germany elected?
en.context: The four-year election cycle provides a steady timeframe for the government to work on long-term projects. It's an exciting time when millions of people shape the future of Germany together!

text: Welche Handlungen mit Bezug auf den Staat Israel sind in Deutschland verboten?

options.b: das Aufhängen einer israelischen Flagge auf dem Privatgrundstück
options.c: eine Diskussion über die Politik Israels
options.d: der öffentliche Aufruf zur Vernichtung Israels

en.text: Which actions regarding the State of Israel are prohibited in Germany?

options.b: flying an Israeli flag on private property
options.d: a public call for the destruction of Israel

en.context: Due to historical responsibility, Germany considers the security of Israel a core part of its own national security. Calling for the destruction of Israel is prohibited to ensure peace and prevent hate speech.

text: Die Wahlen in Deutschland sind …

en.text: Elections in Germany are ...
en.context: Secret ballots ensure that every person can vote according to their own conscience without fear or pressure. In German polling stations, you will always find a private booth to keep your choice hidden!

text: Wahlen in Deutschland gewinnt die Partei, die …

options.b: die meisten Männer mehrheitlich gewählt haben.
options.c: die meisten Stimmen bei den Arbeiterinnen/Arbeitern bekommen hat.
options.d: die meisten Erststimmen für ihre Kanzlerkandidatin/ihren Kanzlerkandidaten erhalten hat.

en.text: In German elections, the party that wins is the one that ...

options.b: was voted for by the majority of men.
options.c: received the most votes from workers.
options.d: received the most first votes for its chancellor candidate.

en.context: This principle ensures that the government reflects the will of the people. Germany's system is designed so that the distribution of seats in parliament closely matches the percentage of votes each party receives.

text: An demokratischen Wahlen in Deutschland teilzunehmen ist …

en.text: Participating in democratic elections in Germany is ...
en.context: Voting is a fundamental right that empowers you to help decide the direction of the country. It is one of the most important ways to make your voice heard in a democracy!

text: Was bedeutet "aktives Wahlrecht" in Deutschland?

options.d: Man muss zur Auszählung der Stimmen gehen.

en.text: What does "active suffrage" mean in Germany?

options.d: You must attend the counting of the votes.

en.context: Active suffrage is your right to cast a vote in an election. In contrast, 'passive suffrage' is the right to actually run as a candidate to be elected to office!

text: Wenn Sie bei einer Bundestagswahl in Deutschland wählen dürfen, heißt das …

en.text: If you are allowed to vote in a German federal election, this is called ...
en.context: Active suffrage is the legal right to cast your vote in an election, while 'passive suffrage' is the right to run as a candidate. It's your powerful tool for shaping the future of the country!

text: Wie viel Prozent der Zweitstimmen müssen Parteien mindestens bekommen, um in den Deutschen Bundestag gewählt zu werden?

en.text: What minimum percentage of second votes must parties receive to be elected to the German Bundestag?
en.context: The 5% threshold prevents the parliament from being split among too many tiny parties, which helps the government stay stable. Did you know this is called the 'five-percent hurdle'?

text: Wer darf bei den rund 40 jüdischen Makkabi-Sportvereinen Mitglied werden?

en.text: Who is allowed to become a member of the approximately 40 Jewish Maccabi sports clubs?
en.context: While these clubs are based on Jewish values and heritage, they welcome everyone regardless of their background. They are fantastic places to meet new people and stay active!

text: Wahlen in Deutschland sind frei. Was bedeutet das?

options.a: Alle verurteilten Straftäterinnen/Straftäter dürfen nicht wählen.
options.b: Wenn ich wählen gehen möchte, muss meine Arbeitgeberin/mein Arbeitgeber mir frei geben.
options.c: Jede Person kann ohne Zwang entscheiden, ob sie wählen möchte und wen sie wählen möchte.
options.d: Ich kann frei entscheiden, wo ich wählen gehen möchte.

en.text: Elections in Germany are free. What does this mean?

options.a: All convicted criminals are not allowed to vote.
options.b: If I want to go vote, my employer must give me time off.
options.c: Every person can decide without coercion whether they want to vote and whom they want to vote for.
options.d: I can freely decide where I want to go to vote.

en.context: In this context, 'free' means you are free from pressure or threats when choosing your candidate. Your vote is your own secret and a vital part of a healthy democracy!

text: Das Wahlsystem in Deutschland ist ein …

en.text: The electoral system in Germany is a ...
en.context: This system combines the best of both worlds: you vote for a person from your local area and for a political party. This ensures that both local needs and national party goals are represented!

text: Eine Partei möchte in den Deutschen Bundestag. Sie muss aber einen Mindestanteil an Wählerstimmen haben. Das heißt …

en.text: A party wants to enter the German Bundestag. However, it must achieve a minimum share of the vote. This is known as the...
en.context: The 5% threshold prevents too many small parties from entering parliament, which helps the government remain stable and efficient. It's like a rule to ensure parties have a significant level of support before they can help make laws!

text: Welchem Grundsatz unterliegen Wahlen in Deutschland? Wahlen in Deutschland sind …

en.text: What principle governs elections in Germany? Elections in Germany are...
en.context: These principles ensure that you can vote without pressure, every single vote carries the same weight, and your choice remains private. This is the golden standard for protecting your democratic rights!

text: Was ist in Deutschland die "5%-Hürde"?

options.a: Abstimmungsregelung im Bundestag für kleine Parteien
options.c: Mindestanteil an Wählerstimmen, um ins Parlament zu kommen

en.text: What is the "5% threshold" in Germany?

options.a: Voting regulation in the Bundestag for small parties
options.b: Attendance check in the Bundestag for votes
options.c: Minimum share of voter votes required to enter parliament
options.d: Attendance check in the Bundesrat for votes

en.context: By requiring at least 5% of the vote, Germany avoids having too many tiny parties, which would make it very hard to form a working government. It ensures that only parties with broad support enter the Bundestag!

text: Die Bundestagswahl in Deutschland ist die Wahl …

en.text: The Bundestag election in Germany is the election...
en.context: The Bundestag is Germany's national parliament where representatives from across the country meet to debate and pass laws. It is the heart of Germany's legislative power!

text: In einer Demokratie ist eine Funktion von regelmäßigen Wahlen, …

options.a: die Bürgerinnen und Bürger zu zwingen, ihre Stimme abzugeben.
options.b: nach dem Willen der Wählermehrheit den Wechsel der Regierung zu ermöglichen.
options.d: den Armen mehr Macht zu geben.

en.text: In a democracy, one function of regular elections is...

options.a: to force citizens to cast their vote.
options.b: to enable a change of government according to the will of the majority of voters.
options.c: to maintain existing laws in the country.
options.d: to give more power to the poor.

en.context: Regular elections give the people the power to decide if they are happy with their leaders or want a fresh start. This peaceful transition of power is what makes a democracy truly work!

text: Was bekommen wahlberechtigte Bürgerinnen und Bürger in Deutschland vor einer Wahl?

options.b: eine Wahlerlaubnis von der Bundespräsidentin/von dem Bundespräsidenten

en.text: What do eligible voters in Germany receive before an election?

options.a: a voting notification from the municipality
options.b: a voting permit from the Federal President
options.c: a notification from the Federal Convention
options.d: a notification from the parish office

en.context: Your municipality sends you a voting notification so you know exactly where and when to cast your vote. Did you know you can use this letter to easily apply for a postal vote if you're busy on election day?

text: Warum gibt es die 5%-Hürde im Wahlgesetz der Bundesrepublik Deutschland? Es gibt sie, weil …

options.a: die Programme von vielen kleinen Parteien viele Gemeinsamkeiten haben.
options.b: die Bürgerinnen und Bürger bei vielen kleinen Parteien die Orientierung verlieren können.
options.c: viele kleine Parteien die Regierungsbildung erschweren.
options.d: die kleinen Parteien nicht so viel Geld haben, um die Politikerinnen und Politiker zu bezahlen.

en.text: Why does the election law of the Federal Republic of Germany include the 5% threshold? It exists because...

options.a: the programs of many small parties share many similarities.
options.b: citizens could lose their orientation with too many small parties.
options.c: many small parties make the formation of a government more difficult.
options.d: small parties do not have enough money to pay their politicians.

en.context: The 5% threshold prevents the parliament from splitting into too many tiny parties, ensuring the government remains stable and effective. This rule was created to avoid the political chaos experienced during the Weimar Republic era.

text: Parlamentsmitglieder, die von den Bürgerinnen und Bürgern gewählt werden, nennt man …

en.text: Members of parliament who are elected by the citizens are called...
en.context: Elected representatives are called members of parliament because they represent the citizens in the legislative process. Interestingly, they are 'representatives of the whole people' and are not legally bound by instructions from their voters!

text: Vom Volk gewählt wird in Deutschland …

en.text: Which of the following is elected by the people in Germany?

options.b: the state premier of a federal state.

en.context: The Bundestag is the heart of German democracy and is the only federal body directly elected by the people every four years. It meets in the iconic Reichstag building, which features a famous glass dome open to the public!

text: Welcher Stimmzettel wäre bei einer Bundestagswahl gültig? In Anlehnung an Bundeswahlordnung (BWO), Anlage 26

en.text: Which ballot would be valid in a Bundestag election? Based on the Federal Electoral Regulations (BWO), Annex 26
en.context: A ballot is valid when the voter's choice is clear and no identifying marks are added, as secrecy is essential for a fair election. To ensure your vote counts, always follow the marking instructions carefully!

text: In Deutschland ist eine Bürgermeisterin/ein Bürgermeister …

en.text: In Germany, a mayor is...
en.context: The mayor (Bürgermeister) leads the local government and manages the city's affairs. Fun fact: In many German municipalities, citizens get to elect their mayor directly!

text: Viele Menschen in Deutschland arbeiten in ihrer Freizeit ehrenamtlich. Was bedeutet das?

options.b: Sie arbeiten freiwillig und unbezahlt in Vereinen und Verbänden.
options.d: Sie arbeiten in einem Krankenhaus und verdienen dabei Geld.

en.text: Many people in Germany do voluntary work in their spare time. What does this mean?

options.b: They work voluntarily and unpaid in clubs and associations.
options.c: They work in the federal government.
options.d: They work in a hospital and earn money.

en.context: Voluntary work, known as 'Ehrenamt,' is a tradition where people give their time to support the community for free. Did you know that many German fire departments rely heavily on these dedicated volunteers?

text: Was ist bei Bundestags- und Landtagswahlen in Deutschland erlaubt?

options.a: Der Ehemann wählt für seine Frau mit.
options.b: Man kann durch Briefwahl seine Stimme abgeben.
options.c: Man kann am Wahltag telefonisch seine Stimme abgeben.
options.d: Kinder ab dem Alter von 14 Jahren dürfen wählen.

en.text: What is permitted during federal and state elections in Germany?

options.a: The husband votes on behalf of his wife.
options.b: You can cast your vote by mail.
options.c: You can cast your vote by telephone on election day.
options.d: Children from the age of 14 are allowed to vote.

en.context: Mail-in voting (Briefwahl) ensures that everyone can exercise their right to vote, even if they are sick or traveling. A cool detail: you can request a postal vote without needing to provide a specific excuse!

text: Man will die Buslinie abschaffen, mit der Sie immer zur Arbeit fahren. Was können Sie machen, um die Buslinie zu erhalten?

options.a: Ich beteilige mich an einer Bürgerinitiative für die Erhaltung der Buslinie oder gründe selber eine Initiative.
options.b: Ich werde Mitglied in einem Sportverein und trainiere Radfahren.
options.c: Ich wende mich an das Finanzamt, weil ich als Steuerzahlerin/Steuerzahler ein Recht auf die Buslinie habe.
options.d: Ich schreibe einen Brief an das Forstamt der Gemeinde.

en.text: There are plans to abolish the bus line you always take to work. What can you do to save the bus line?

options.a: I participate in a citizens' initiative to preserve the bus line or start one myself.
options.b: I join a sports club and train in cycling.
options.c: I contact the tax office because, as a taxpayer, I have a right to the bus line.
options.d: I write a letter to the municipality's forestry office.

en.context: Citizens' initiatives allow you to team up with others to influence local politics and protect things you care about. It's a fantastic way to actively shape the neighborhood where you live!

text: Wen vertreten die Gewerkschaften in Deutschland?

en.text: Whom do trade unions represent in Germany?
en.context: Trade unions act as a collective voice for employees to negotiate fair wages and safe working conditions. This cooperation helps keep the German economy stable and fair for workers across all industries!

text: Sie gehen in Deutschland zum Arbeitsgericht bei …

options.b: ungerechtfertigter Kündigung durch Ihre Chefin/Ihren Chef.

en.text: In Germany, you go to the labor court in case of...

options.b: an unjustified dismissal by your boss.

en.context: Labor courts (Arbeitsgerichte) are specialized courts designed to ensure fairness and protect employees from unfair treatment. A helpful tip: in the first instance of a labor court case, each party usually pays for their own lawyer, regardless of who wins!

text: Welches Gericht ist in Deutschland bei Konflikten in der Arbeitswelt zuständig?

en.text: Which court in Germany is responsible for conflicts in the workplace?
en.context: Germany uses specialized courts to handle specific types of disputes, and the labor court is the dedicated place for workplace issues. This ensures that the judges have deep expertise in employment law to help you!

text: Was kann ich in Deutschland machen, wenn mir meine Arbeitgeberin/mein Arbeitgeber zu Unrecht gekündigt hat?

options.a: weiterarbeiten und freundlich zur Chefin/zum Chef sein
options.b: ein Mahnverfahren gegen die Arbeitgeberin/den Arbeitgeber führen
options.d: die Arbeitgeberin/den Arbeitgeber bei der Polizei anzeigen

en.text: What can I do in Germany if my employer has wrongfully terminated my employment?

options.a: continue working and be friendly to the boss
options.b: initiate debt collection proceedings against the employer
options.c: file a lawsuit against unfair dismissal
options.d: report the employer to the police

en.context: If you believe your dismissal was illegal, you can file a 'Kündigungsschutzklage' to challenge it. Remember, you usually have a very tight deadline of only three weeks to file this lawsuit!

text: Wann kommt es in Deutschland zu einem Prozess vor Gericht? Wenn jemand …

options.b: eine Straftat begangen hat und angeklagt wird.
options.c: eine andere Meinung als die der Regierung vertritt.
options.d: sein Auto falsch geparkt hat und es abgeschleppt wird.

en.text: When does a court trial take place in Germany? When someone...

options.b: has committed a crime and is indicted.
options.c: holds a different opinion than the government.
options.d: has parked their car incorrectly and it is towed.

en.context: A trial occurs after an indictment, meaning a prosecutor has found enough evidence to bring the case before a judge. This process is designed to protect the innocent while ensuring justice is served.

text: Was macht eine Schöffin/ein Schöffe in Deutschland? Sie/Er …

options.a: entscheidet mit Richterinnen/Richtern über Schuld und Strafe.

en.text: What does a lay judge do in Germany? He/she...

options.a: decides on guilt and sentencing together with professional judges.

en.context: Lay judges (Schöffen) are ordinary citizens who help professional judges make decisions, ensuring that the perspective of the general public is heard. It is a wonderful way for everyday people to participate in the German legal system!

text: Wer berät in Deutschland Personen bei Rechtsfragen und vertritt sie vor Gericht?

en.text: Who advises people on legal matters and represents them in court in Germany?
en.context: Lawyers are trained experts who guide you through the legal system and speak for you in court to protect your rights. Fun fact: You can often find low-cost legal aid (Beratungshilfe) if you have a low income!

text: Was ist die Hauptaufgabe einer Richterin/eines Richters in Deutschland? Eine Richterin/ein Richter …

options.a: vertritt Bürgerinnen und Bürger vor einem Gericht.
options.b: arbeitet an einem Gericht und spricht Urteile.

en.text: What is the main task of a judge in Germany? A judge...

options.b: works at a court and delivers judgments.

en.context: Judges act as impartial referees who study the evidence and deliver a final decision based on the law. Remember, German judges are independent and cannot be ordered by politicians to rule a certain way!

text: Eine Richterin/ein Richter in Deutschland gehört zur …

en.text: A judge in Germany belongs to the...
en.context: The judiciary consists of all the courts and judges who interpret the law to ensure justice for everyone. This system helps keep the legal process fair and consistent across the whole country.

text: Eine Richterin/ein Richter gehört in Deutschland zur …

en.text: In Germany, a judge is part of the...
en.context: Judicial power is one of the three main pillars of the state, acting as a check and balance against the other branches. This ensures that no single part of government becomes too powerful!

text: In Deutschland wird die Staatsgewalt geteilt. Für welche Staatsgewalt arbeitet eine Richterin/ein Richter? Für die …

en.text: In Germany, state power is divided. For which branch of state power does a judge work? The...
en.context: The judiciary is the branch responsible for applying laws to real-life situations in court. In German, this division of power is called 'Gewaltenteilung,' which is a fundamental heart of German democracy.

text: Wie nennt man in Deutschland ein Verfahren vor einem Gericht?

en.text: What is a court proceeding called in Germany?
en.context: A trial is the formal process where evidence is presented in court to reach a legal decision. In Germany, most trials are open to the public to ensure transparency and fairness!

text: Was ist die Arbeit einer Richterin/eines Richters in Deutschland?

en.text: What is the job of a judge in Germany?
en.context: Judges are independent officials responsible for applying the law fairly to every case. To keep them impartial, German judges are appointed for life, meaning they can make decisions without fearing political pressure.

text: Was ist eine Aufgabe der Polizei in Deutschland?

options.d: die Einhaltung von Gesetzen zu überwachen

en.text: What is a task of the police in Germany?
en.context: The police maintain public safety by ensuring that laws are followed by everyone. Interestingly, police authority in Germany is primarily managed by the individual states (Länder) rather than one single central office.

text: Was ist ein Beispiel für antisemitisches Verhalten?

en.text: What is an example of antisemitic behavior?
en.context: Antisemitism is prejudice toward Jewish people, and denying the Holocaust is a severe form of this. In Germany, Holocaust denial is a criminal offense because the country is deeply committed to remembering history and protecting human dignity.

text: Eine Gerichtsschöffin/ein Gerichtsschöffe in Deutschland ist …

options.d: eine Person, die Jura studiert hat.

en.text: A lay judge in Germany is...

options.a: the deputy of the city's head.
options.c: a member of a municipal council.
options.d: a person who has studied law.

en.context: Lay judges are ordinary citizens who serve as honorary judges to provide a community perspective in court. This is a wonderful way for regular people to participate directly in the democratic legal system!

text: Wer baute die Mauer in Berlin?

en.text: Who built the Wall in Berlin?
en.context: The GDR (East Germany) built the wall in 1961 to prevent its citizens from fleeing to the West. It stood for 28 years before finally falling in 1989, bringing families back together!

text: Wann waren die Nationalsozialisten mit Adolf Hitler in Deutschland an der Macht?

en.text: When were the National Socialists, with Adolf Hitler, in power in Germany?
en.context: This dark period of German history lasted from 1933 until the defeat of the regime in 1945. Today, Germany teaches this history deeply to ensure such events never happen again.

text: Was war am 8. Mai 1945?

options.c: Wahl von Konrad Adenauer zum Bundeskanzler
options.d: Ende des Zweiten Weltkriegs in Europa

en.text: What happened on May 8, 1945?

options.b: Start of the construction of the Berlin Wall
options.c: Election of Konrad Adenauer as Federal Chancellor
options.d: End of the Second World War in Europe

en.context: May 8, 1945, marks the day Nazi Germany surrendered, officially ending the war in Europe. This date represents a vital turning point toward the peace and democracy Germany enjoys today.

text: Wann war der Zweite Weltkrieg zu Ende?

en.text: When did the Second World War end?
en.context: The Second World War ended in 1945, bringing an end to a global conflict of immense scale. Following the war, the United Nations was established to foster global cooperation and prevent future wars.

text: Wann waren die Nationalsozialisten in Deutschland an der Macht?

en.text: When were the National Socialists in power in Germany?
en.context: The National Socialists held power from 1933 to 1945, a time of dictatorship and persecution. Remembering this era is a key part of Germany's 'culture of remembrance' to protect human rights for everyone.

text: In welchem Jahr wurde Hitler Reichskanzler?

en.text: In which year did Hitler become Reich Chancellor?
en.context: Hitler was appointed Reich Chancellor in 1933, marking the end of the Weimar Republic. Today, Germany's constitution is specifically designed to prevent any single person from gaining that kind of power again!

text: Die Nationalsozialisten mit Adolf Hitler errichteten 1933 in Deutschland …

en.text: In 1933, the National Socialists, with Adolf Hitler, established ... in Germany.
en.context: The National Socialists replaced democracy with a dictatorship, meaning all power rested with one leader. It's a powerful lesson in why we value the 'checks and balances' in modern governments!

text: Das "Dritte Reich" war eine …

en.text: The "Third Reich" was a ...
en.context: The 'Third Reich' was the name given to the Nazi dictatorship that ruled Germany from 1933 to 1945. You'll find that modern Germany is now a global leader in promoting human rights and democracy.

text: Was gab es in Deutschland nicht während der Zeit des Nationalsozialismus?

en.text: What did not exist in Germany during the era of National Socialism?
en.context: Free elections disappeared during this time because the regime wanted to silence all opposing voices. In modern Germany, free and fair elections are the heart of the democratic process!

text: Welcher Krieg dauerte von 1939 bis 1945?

en.text: Which war lasted from 1939 to 1945?
en.context: The Second World War began in 1939 and ended in 1945, leaving a deep mark on the world. This shared history is why Germany works so closely with its neighbors in the European Union today.

text: Was kennzeichnete den NS-Staat? Eine Politik …

en.text: What characterized the Nazi state? A policy of...
en.context: The Nazi state used state racism to justify the systemic persecution and genocide of millions of people. Today, Germany actively teaches this history to ensure that equality and human dignity are always protected.

text: Claus Schenk Graf von Stauffenberg wurde bekannt durch …

options.a: eine Goldmedaille bei den Olympischen Spielen 1936.
options.d: das Attentat auf Hitler am 20. Juli 1944.

en.text: Claus Schenk Graf von Stauffenberg became known for...

options.a: a gold medal at the 1936 Olympic Games.
options.b: the construction of the Reichstag building.
options.d: the assassination attempt on Hitler on July 20, 1944.

en.context: Stauffenberg led a courageous attempt to stop Hitler's regime from the inside to end the war. This event is remembered as a powerful symbol of the internal resistance against the Nazi dictatorship.

text: In welchem Jahr zerstörten die Nationalsozialisten Synagogen und jüdische Geschäfte in Deutschland?

en.text: In which year did the Nazis destroy synagogues and Jewish businesses in Germany?
en.context: 1938 was a devastating year when the Nazi regime shifted toward open, violent persecution of Jewish people. Remembering these dates helps us understand the vital importance of standing up against hate.

text: Was passierte am 9. November 1938 in Deutschland?

options.a: Mit dem Angriff auf Polen beginnt der Zweite Weltkrieg.
options.b: Die Nationalsozialisten verlieren eine Wahl und lösen den Reichstag auf.
options.c: Jüdische Geschäfte und Synagogen werden durch Nationalsozialisten und ihre Anhänger zerstört.
options.d: Hitler wird Reichspräsident und lässt alle Parteien verbieten.

en.text: What happened on November 9, 1938, in Germany?

options.a: World War II begins with the attack on Poland.
options.b: The Nazis lose an election and dissolve the Reichstag.
