options.c: Jewish businesses and synagogues are destroyed by Nazis and their supporters.
options.d: Hitler becomes Reich President and bans all political parties.

en.context: November 9, 1938, is known as 'Kristallnacht' (the Night of Broken Glass) due to the shattered windows of Jewish shops. It serves as a stark reminder of the horrors of the Holocaust and the need for tolerance.

text: Wie hieß der erste Bundeskanzler der Bundesrepublik Deutschland?

en.text: Who was the first Federal Chancellor of the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: Konrad Adenauer guided Germany through its early years of democracy and helped integrate the country into the European community. He is often credited with the 'economic miracle' that helped rebuild Germany's prosperity.

text: Bei welchen Demonstrationen in Deutschland riefen die Menschen "Wir sind das Volk"?

options.b: bei den Demonstrationen 1968 in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland
options.c: bei den Anti-Atomkraft-Demonstrationen 1985 in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland
options.d: bei den Montagsdemonstrationen 1989 in der DDR

en.text: During which demonstrations in Germany did people chant "We are the people"?

options.a: during the 1953 workers' uprising in the GDR
options.b: during the 1968 demonstrations in the Federal Republic of Germany
options.c: during the anti-nuclear power demonstrations in 1985 in the Federal Republic of Germany
options.d: during the Monday demonstrations in 1989 in the GDR

en.context: The Monday demonstrations in 1989 were peaceful protests in East Germany that led to the fall of the Berlin Wall. A cool detail: the chant later changed to 'We are ONE people' to show the desire for reunification!

text: Welche Länder wurden nach dem Zweiten Weltkrieg in Deutschland als "Alliierte Besatzungsmächte" bezeichnet?

en.text: Which countries were referred to as the "Allied occupying powers" in Germany after World War II?

options.a: Soviet Union, Great Britain, Poland, Sweden
options.d: USA, Soviet Union, Great Britain, France

en.context: After World War II, these four nations divided Germany into occupation zones to ensure peace and rebuild the country. This cooperation laid the early groundwork for the modern political structure of Europe!

text: Welches Land war keine "Alliierte Besatzungsmacht" in Deutschland?

en.text: Which country was not an "Allied occupying power" in Germany?
en.context: Japan was an ally of Germany during WWII, not one of the victorious Allied powers who managed the occupation. Only the USA, Soviet Union, Great Britain, and France held that role.

text: Wann wurde die Bundesrepublik Deutschland gegründet?

en.text: When was the Federal Republic of Germany founded?
en.context: The Federal Republic of Germany was founded in 1949 to establish a stable, democratic government in the West. Interestingly, the GDR (East Germany) was also founded in that same pivotal year!

text: Was gab es während der Zeit des Nationalsozialismus in Deutschland?

options.b: das Recht zur freien Entfaltung der Persönlichkeit

en.text: What existed during the period of National Socialism in Germany?

options.b: the right to the free development of personality

en.context: The Nazi regime banned all other political parties to establish a totalitarian dictatorship and absolute control. This dark history is why Germany today strongly protects political pluralism and democratic freedom.

text: Soziale Marktwirtschaft bedeutet, die Wirtschaft …

options.a: steuert sich allein nach Angebot und Nachfrage.
options.b: wird vom Staat geplant und gesteuert, Angebot und Nachfrage werden nicht berücksichtigt.
options.c: richtet sich nach der Nachfrage im Ausland.
options.d: richtet sich nach Angebot und Nachfrage, aber der Staat sorgt für einen sozialen Ausgleich.

en.text: A social market economy means that the economy...

options.a: is governed solely by supply and demand.
options.b: is planned and controlled by the state, and supply and demand are not considered.
options.d: is based on supply and demand, but the state ensures social equity.

en.context: The social market economy blends free-market competition with social safeguards to protect all citizens. It's often called the 'German Model' because it seeks a fair balance between profit and social welfare!

text: In welcher Besatzungszone wurde die DDR gegründet? In der …

en.text: In which occupation zone was the GDR founded? In the...
en.context: The GDR was established in the Soviet occupation zone, which eventually became East Germany. This period of division is a key part of modern German history and the Cold War era!

text: Die Bundesrepublik Deutschland ist ein Gründungsmitglied …

en.text: The Federal Republic of Germany is a founding member...

options.a: of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO).

en.context: Germany helped found the European Union to promote lasting peace and economic cooperation after the war. Today, the EU makes it easy for millions of people to work and travel freely across borders!

text: Wann wurde die DDR gegründet?

en.text: When was the GDR founded?
en.context: The GDR was founded in 1949, the same year as the Federal Republic of Germany. For 40 years, these two states operated under very different political and economic systems!

text: Wie viele Besatzungszonen gab es in Deutschland nach dem Zweiten Weltkrieg?

en.text: How many occupation zones were there in Germany after the Second World War?
en.context: After World War II, the Allies divided Germany into four zones managed by the USA, UK, France, and the Soviet Union. Interestingly, the city of Berlin was also split into four sectors!

text: Wie waren die Besatzungszonen Deutschlands nach 1945 verteilt?

en.text: How were Germany's occupation zones distributed after 1945?
en.context: After WWII, the four Allied powers divided Germany into zones to manage its reconstruction and ensure peace. It's interesting to note that these zones later shaped the boundary between East and West Germany!

text: Welche deutsche Stadt wurde nach dem Zweiten Weltkrieg in vier Sektoren aufgeteilt?

en.text: Which German city was divided into four sectors after the Second World War?
en.context: Because of its central importance, Berlin was divided into four sectors despite being located deep within the Soviet zone. This made the city a global symbol of the Cold War for decades.

text: Vom Juni 1948 bis zum Mai 1949 wurden die Bürgerinnen und Bürger von West-Berlin durch eine Luftbrücke versorgt. Welcher Umstand war dafür verantwortlich?

options.a: Für Frankreich war eine Versorgung der West-Berliner Bevölkerung mit dem Flugzeug kostengünstiger.
options.b: Die amerikanischen Soldatinnen und Soldaten hatten beim Landtransport Angst vor Überfällen.
options.c: Für Großbritannien war die Versorgung über die Luftbrücke schneller.
options.d: Die Sowjetunion unterbrach den gesamten Verkehr auf dem Landwege.

en.text: From June 1948 to May 1949, the citizens of West Berlin were supplied by an airlift. Which circumstance was responsible for this?

options.a: For France, supplying the population of West Berlin by plane was more cost-effective.
options.b: American soldiers feared attacks during land transport.
options.c: For Great Britain, supply via the airlift was faster.
options.d: The Soviet Union blocked all land transport.

en.context: The Berlin Airlift was a heroic effort to supply the city after the Soviet Union blocked all land access. A sweet detail: some pilots dropped candy for children using small parachutes!

text: Wie endete der Zweite Weltkrieg in Europa offiziell?

options.c: mit dem Rückzug der Deutschen aus den besetzten Gebieten

en.text: How did the Second World War in Europe officially end?

options.a: with the death of Adolf Hitler
options.b: through the unconditional surrender of Germany
options.c: with the withdrawal of Germans from the occupied territories

en.context: World War II ended when Germany surrendered unconditionally, ensuring the Nazi regime was completely dismantled. This pivotal moment paved the way for the democratic Germany we know today.

text: Der erste Bundeskanzler der Bundesrepublik Deutschland war …

en.text: The first Federal Chancellor of the Federal Republic of Germany was...
en.context: Konrad Adenauer was the first Chancellor of West Germany and played a key role in rebuilding the nation after the war. He is remembered for bringing Germany back into the global community!

text: Was wollte Willy Brandt mit seinem Kniefall 1970 im ehemaligen jüdischen Ghetto in Warschau ausdrücken?

options.a: Er hat sich den ehemaligen Alliierten unterworfen.
options.b: Er bat Polen und die polnischen Juden um Vergebung.
options.c: Er zeigte seine Demut vor dem Warschauer Pakt.
options.d: Er sprach ein Gebet am Grab des Unbekannten Soldaten.

en.text: What did Willy Brandt want to convey with his act of kneeling in the former Jewish ghetto in Warsaw in 1970?

options.a: He submitted to the former Allies.
options.b: He asked Poland and the Polish Jews for forgiveness.
options.c: He showed his humility before the Warsaw Pact.
options.d: He said a prayer at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier.

en.context: This powerful gesture symbolized Germany's request for forgiveness for the horrors of the Holocaust. It is remembered today as a key moment in the healing of relations between Germany and Poland!

text: Wie heißt das jüdische Gebetshaus?

en.text: What is the Jewish house of prayer called?
en.context: A synagogue is the sacred space where Jewish communities gather for prayer and the study of the Torah. Many cities in Germany have beautiful synagogues that serve as vibrant community centers!

text: Wann war in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland das "Wirtschaftswunder"?

en.text: When did the "economic miracle" occur in the Federal Republic of Germany?
en.context: The 'Wirtschaftswunder' saw West Germany transform from a war-torn nation into a leading global economy. A fun fact: the iconic Volkswagen Beetle became a global symbol of this period's success!

text: 184. Auf welcher rechtlichen Grundlage wurde der Staat Israel gegründet?

en.text: On what legal basis was the State of Israel founded?

options.b: a decision of the Zionist Congress
options.c: a proposal by the Federal Government

en.context: The Federal Government's legal and diplomatic support has been crucial in recognizing and securing the State of Israel. This commitment remains a cornerstone of Germany's foreign policy and responsibility today.

text: Wofür stand der Ausdruck "Eiserner Vorhang"? Für die Abschottung …

options.a: des Warschauer Pakts gegen den Westen

en.text: What did the expression "Iron Curtain" stand for? For the isolation of...

options.a: the Warsaw Pact from the West

en.context: The 'Iron Curtain' was a political boundary that divided Europe into the democratic West and the communist East during the Cold War. Its fall in 1989 paved the way for the reunification of Germany!

text: Im Jahr 1953 gab es in der DDR einen Aufstand, an den lange Zeit in der Bundesrepublik Deutschland ein Feiertag erinnerte. Wann war das?

en.text: In 1953, there was an uprising in the GDR that was commemorated for a long time by a public holiday in the Federal Republic of Germany. When was this?
en.context: June 17th marks the workers' uprising of 1953 against the GDR government. West Germany honored this bravery as a national holiday for many years to show solidarity!

text: Welcher deutsche Staat hatte eine schwarz-rot-goldene Flagge mit Hammer, Zirkel und Ährenkranz?

en.text: Which German state had a black-red-gold flag with a hammer, compass, and wreath of grain?
en.context: The GDR's flag featured a hammer, compass, and grain to represent the unity of workers, intellectuals, and farmers. It's a fascinating look at how socialist values were visualized!

text: In welchem Jahr wurde die Mauer in Berlin gebaut?

en.text: In which year was the wall built in Berlin?
en.context: The Berlin Wall was built in 1961 to prevent people from leaving East Berlin. It remains a poignant reminder of how dividing a city can impact millions of lives.

text: Wann baute die DDR die Mauer in Berlin?

en.text: When did the GDR build the wall in Berlin?
en.context: In 1961, the GDR constructed the wall, which became a global symbol of the 'Iron Curtain.' Interestingly, the wall was finally opened on November 9, 1989!

text: Was bedeutet die Abkürzung DDR?

en.text: What does the abbreviation GDR stand for?
en.context: GDR stands for the German Democratic Republic, the state that governed East Germany from 1949 until reunification. Learning these terms helps you understand Germany's journey back to being one country!

text: Wann wurde die Mauer in Berlin für alle geöffnet?

en.text: When was the Berlin Wall opened to everyone?
en.context: The Berlin Wall fell in November 1989, symbolizing the end of the Cold War and the reuniting of families. It's a powerful reminder of the longing for freedom!

text: Welches heutige deutsche Bundesland gehörte früher zum Gebiet der DDR?

en.text: Which of today's German federal states was previously part of the GDR?
en.context: Brandenburg is one of the five states that were part of the former East Germany (GDR). Interestingly, this state completely surrounds the city of Berlin!

text: Von 1961 bis 1989 war Berlin …

en.text: From 1961 to 1989, Berlin was …
en.context: From 1961 to 1989, a concrete wall physically separated East and West Berlin. Today, you can visit the East Side Gallery to see beautiful murals painted on the remaining sections of the wall.

text: Am 3. Oktober feiert man in Deutschland den Tag der Deutschen …

en.text: On October 3rd, Germany celebrates the Day of German …
en.context: October 3rd celebrates the official reunification of East and West Germany in 1990. It's a national holiday where people all over the country celebrate togetherness and peace!

text: Welches heutige deutsche Bundesland gehörte früher zum Gebiet der DDR?

en.text: Which of today's German federal states was previously part of the GDR?
en.context: Saxony-Anhalt was part of the former GDR and is now a vibrant federal state in eastern Germany. It is home to the famous Bauhaus school, which revolutionized modern architecture and design!

text: Warum nennt man die Zeit im Herbst 1989 in der DDR "Die Wende"? In dieser Zeit veränderte sich die DDR politisch …

options.b: von einer liberalen Marktwirtschaft zum Sozialismus.
options.d: von einem religiösen Staat zu einem kommunistischen Staat.

en.text: Why is the period in the autumn of 1989 in the GDR called "Die Wende" (the turn)? During this time, the GDR changed politically …

options.a: from a dictatorship to a democracy.
options.b: from a liberal market economy to socialism.
options.c: from a monarchy to social democracy.
options.d: from a religious state to a communist state.

en.context: 'Die Wende' refers to the peaceful revolution where East Germans demanded freedom, leading the GDR to shift from a dictatorship to a democracy. It is a powerful example of how citizens can create positive change through peaceful protest!

text: Welches heutige deutsche Bundesland gehörte früher zum Gebiet der DDR?

en.text: Which of today's German federal states was previously part of the GDR?
en.context: Thuringia is one of the 'new federal states' that joined the Federal Republic of Germany during reunification in 1990. A fun fact: Thuringia is often called the 'green heart' of Germany because of its lush forests!

text: Welches heutige deutsche Bundesland gehörte früher zum Gebiet der DDR?

en.text: Which of today's German federal states was previously part of the GDR?
en.context: Saxony was a major part of the former GDR and is now a state known for its rich industrial and artistic history. You'll find the beautiful city of Dresden here, often called the 'Florence on the Elbe'!

text: Mit der Abkürzung "Stasi" meinte man in der DDR …

en.text: In the GDR, the abbreviation "Stasi" referred to …
en.context: The Stasi was the GDR's secret police, tasked with monitoring citizens to maintain the dictatorship's power. Today, the Stasi Records Agency allows people to see the files kept on them, ensuring this history is never forgotten.

text: Welches heutige deutsche Bundesland gehörte früher zum Gebiet der DDR?

en.text: Which of today's German federal states was previously part of the GDR?
en.context: Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania is a beautiful coastal state in the northeast that was previously part of the GDR. It's famous for its stunning Baltic Sea beaches and relaxing island landscapes!

text: Welche der folgenden Auflistungen enthält nur Bundesländer, die zum Gebiet der früheren DDR gehörten?

en.text: Which of the following lists contains only federal states that were part of the former GDR?

options.a: Lower Saxony, North Rhine-Westphalia, Hesse, Schleswig-Holstein, Brandenburg
options.b: Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania, Brandenburg, Saxony, Saxony-Anhalt, Thuringia
options.d: Saxony, Thuringia, Hesse, Lower Saxony, Brandenburg

en.context: These five 'new federal states' comprise the territory of the former East Germany. A great way to remember them is that they all share a rich history of art, culture, and industry!

text: Zu wem gehörte die DDR im "Kalten Krieg"?

en.text: Which side did the GDR belong to during the "Cold War"?
en.context: During the Cold War, the GDR was a socialist state closely allied with the Soviet Union through the Warsaw Pact. This pact was essentially the counterpart to the Western-led NATO alliance.

text: Wie hieß das Wirtschaftssystem der DDR?

en.text: What was the economic system of the GDR called?
en.context: In a planned economy, the state decides what is produced and at what price, rather than letting the market decide. This is very different from the social market economy that Germany uses today!

text: Wie wurden die Bundesrepublik Deutschland und die DDR zu einem Staat?

options.a: Die Bundesrepublik hat die DDR besetzt.
options.b: Die heutigen fünf östlichen Bundesländer sind der Bundesrepublik Deutschland beigetreten.
options.c: Die westlichen Bundesländer sind der DDR beigetreten.
options.d: Die DDR hat die Bundesrepublik Deutschland besetzt.

en.text: How did the Federal Republic of Germany and the GDR become one state?

options.a: The Federal Republic occupied the GDR.
options.b: The five current eastern federal states joined the Federal Republic of Germany.
options.c: The western federal states joined the GDR.
options.d: The GDR occupied the Federal Republic of Germany.

en.context: Germany was reunited on October 3, 1990, when the eastern states officially joined the Federal Republic. This historic event is celebrated every year as the Day of German Unity!

text: Mit dem Beitritt der DDR zur Bundesrepublik Deutschland gehören die neuen Bundesländer nun auch …

en.text: With the accession of the GDR to the Federal Republic of Germany, the new federal states now also belong...
en.context: Because the Federal Republic of Germany was already a member, the eastern states automatically became part of the European Union upon reunification. This ensures that all Germans enjoy the same EU rights, like freedom of movement!

text: 206. Woran erinnern die sogenannten „Stolpersteine“ in Deutschland?

options.a: an berühmte deutsche Politikerinnen und Politiker

en.text: What do the so-called "Stolpersteine" (stumbling stones) in Germany commemorate?
en.context: These small brass plaques are placed in the sidewalk to honor the memory of people persecuted by the Nazis. It is a touching way to keep their names alive in the neighborhoods where they once lived!

text: In welchem Militärbündnis war die DDR Mitglied?

en.text: In which military alliance was the GDR a member?
en.context: During the Cold War, the GDR was part of the Warsaw Pact, a military alliance led by the Soviet Union. This was the opposite side of the NATO alliance, which the FRG joined.

text: Was war die "Stasi"?

options.d: ein deutscher Sportverein während des Zweiten Weltkrieges

en.text: What was the "Stasi"?

options.a: the intelligence service in the "Third Reich"
options.c: the intelligence service of the GDR
options.d: a German sports club during the Second World War

en.context: The Stasi was the state security service of the GDR, feared for its massive system of surveillance and secret informants. Today, you can visit the Stasi Museum in Berlin to learn more about this dark chapter.

text: Welches war das Wappen der Deutschen Demokratischen Republik?

en.text: Which one was the coat of arms of the German Democratic Republic?
en.context: The GDR coat of arms featured a hammer and a compass, symbolizing the unity of workers and intellectuals. It was surrounded by a wreath of rye to represent the farmers.

text: Was ereignete sich am 17. Juni 1953 in der DDR?

options.a: der feierliche Beitritt zum Warschauer Pakt

en.text: What happened on June 17, 1953, in the GDR?

options.a: the ceremonial accession to the Warsaw Pact
options.b: nationwide strikes and a popular uprising
options.d: the first visit of Fidel Castro

en.context: What started as a strike by construction workers turned into a nationwide uprising against the GDR government. It remains a powerful symbol of the people's longing for freedom and democracy.

text: Welcher Politiker steht für die "Ostverträge"?

en.text: Which politician is associated with the 'Eastern Treaties'?
en.context: Willy Brandt is remembered for his 'Ostpolitik,' which aimed to normalize relations with Eastern Europe during the Cold War. He was even awarded the Nobel Peace Prize for his courage and diplomacy!

text: Wie heißt Deutschland mit vollem Namen?

en.text: What is the full name of Germany?
en.context: The official name 'Federal Republic of Germany' shows that the country is a federation of 16 states. It's a great way to remember how power is shared across the country!

text: Wie viele Einwohner hat Deutschland?

en.text: What is the population of Germany?
en.context: With around 84 million people, Germany is the most populous country in the European Union. This large population helps make Germany a global leader in industry and innovation!

text: Welche Farben hat die deutsche Flagge?

en.text: What are the colors of the German flag?
en.context: The black, red, and gold colors stand for democracy and freedom. These colors first became symbols of unity and liberty during the early 19th century!

text: Wer wird als "Kanzler der Deutschen Einheit" bezeichnet?

en.text: Who is referred to as the 'Chancellor of German Unity'?
en.context: Helmut Kohl earned this title because he skillfully navigated the reunification of East and West Germany in 1990. He holds the record for being the longest-serving Chancellor in German history!

text: Welches Symbol ist im Plenarsaal des Deutschen Bundestages zu sehen?

en.text: Which symbol can be seen in the plenary chamber of the German Bundestag?

options.b: the flag of the city of Berlin

en.context: The Federal Eagle is the official national symbol of Germany and represents the state's sovereignty. You can spot it not only in the Bundestag but also on official passports!

text: In welchem Zeitraum gab es die Deutsche Demokratische Republik (DDR)?

en.text: During which period did the German Democratic Republic (GDR) exist?
en.context: The GDR existed from 1949 until the fall of the Berlin Wall led to reunification in 1990. It's a powerful reminder of how Germany overcame a deep division to become one nation again.

text: Wie viele Bundesländer kamen bei der Wiedervereinigung 1990 zur Bundesrepublik Deutschland hinzu?

en.text: How many federal states joined the Federal Republic of Germany during reunification in 1990?
en.context: Five new states joined the Federal Republic in 1990, bringing the total to 16. This historic merger is celebrated every year on October 3rd as the Day of German Unity!

text: Die Bundesrepublik Deutschland hat die Grenzen von heute seit …

en.text: The Federal Republic of Germany has had its current borders since...
en.context: Germany's current borders were established in 1990 after the reunification of East and West Germany. This event officially ended the division of the country after decades of separation.

text: Der 27. Januar ist in Deutschland ein offizieller Gedenktag. Woran erinnert dieser Tag?

options.a: an das Ende des Zweiten Weltkrieges
options.d: an die Opfer des Nationalsozialismus (Tag der Befreiung des Vernichtungslagers Auschwitz)

en.text: January 27th is an official day of remembrance in Germany. What does this day commemorate?

options.a: the end of the Second World War
options.b: the adoption of the Basic Law
options.d: the victims of National Socialism (Day of Liberation of the Auschwitz extermination camp)

en.context: This day is dedicated to remembering the millions of victims of the Holocaust to ensure such history is never repeated. It is a solemn day of reflection and a commitment to human rights for all.

text: Deutschland ist Mitglied des Schengener Abkommens. Was bedeutet das?

options.a: Deutsche können in viele Länder Europas ohne Passkontrolle reisen.
options.b: Alle Menschen können ohne Personenkontrolle in Deutschland einreisen.
options.c: Deutsche können ohne Passkontrolle in jedes Land reisen.
options.d: Deutsche können in jedem Land mit dem Euro bezahlen.

en.text: Germany is a member of the Schengen Agreement. What does this mean?

options.a: Germans can travel to many European countries without passport checks.
options.b: Everyone can enter Germany without identification checks.
options.c: Germans can travel to any country without passport checks.
options.d: Germans can pay with the Euro in every country.

en.context: The Schengen Agreement removes internal border checks, making travel across many European countries fast and easy. It's like having one giant open door to explore the continent!

text: Welches Land ist ein Nachbarland von Deutschland?

en.text: Which country is a neighbor of Germany?
en.context: Switzerland is located to the south of Germany. Fun fact: Both countries share the breathtaking peaks of the Alps!

text: Welches Land ist ein Nachbarland von Deutschland?

en.text: Which country is a neighbor of Germany?
en.context: Poland is Germany's neighbor to the east. This shared border is a key link for travel and trade across Central Europe!

text: Was bedeutet die Abkürzung EU?

en.text: What does the abbreviation EU stand for?
en.context: The European Union is a partnership of countries working together for peace and prosperity. It's why many European nations use the same currency, the Euro!

text: In welchem anderen Land gibt es eine große deutschsprachige Bevölkerung?

en.text: In which other country is there a large German-speaking population?
en.context: Austria is a neighboring country where German is the official language. You'll find that while the language is the same, the Austrian accent is often described as very melodic!

text: Welche ist die Flagge der Europäischen Union?

en.text: Which one is the flag of the European Union?
en.context: The EU flag features 12 gold stars on a blue background, symbolizing unity and harmony among the peoples of Europe. Interestingly, the number 12 represents perfection and completeness, not the number of member states!

text: Welches Land ist ein Nachbarland von Deutschland?

en.text: Which country is a neighbor of Germany?
en.context: Denmark is Germany's neighbor to the north, sharing a peaceful and friendly border. You'll find that many people in Northern Germany and Denmark share very similar cultural and linguistic ties!

text: Wie wird der Beitritt der DDR zur Bundesrepublik Deutschland im Jahr 1990 allgemein genannt?

en.text: What is the accession of the GDR to the Federal Republic of Germany in 1990 generally called?
en.context: German Reunification refers to the historic moment in 1990 when East and West Germany became one single country again. This joyful event is celebrated every year as a national holiday on October 3rd!

text: Welches Land ist ein Nachbarland von Deutschland?

en.text: Which country is a neighbor of Germany?
en.context: Luxembourg is a small but influential neighbor located to the west of Germany. Despite its size, it is a major global financial center and a founding member of the European Union!

text: Das Europäische Parlament wird regelmäßig gewählt, nämlich alle …

en.text: The European Parliament is elected regularly, specifically every...
en.context: Members of the European Parliament are elected every five years to ensure the EU remains democratic and reflects the will of its citizens. This is your chance to help shape the future of Europe!

text: Was bedeutet der Begriff "europäische Integration"?

options.a: Damit sind amerikanische Einwanderinnen und Einwanderer in Europa gemeint.
options.b: Der Begriff meint den Einwanderungsstopp nach Europa.
options.c: Damit sind europäische Auswanderinnen und Auswanderer in den USA gemeint.
options.d: Der Begriff meint den Zusammenschluss europäischer Staaten zur EU.

en.text: What does the term "European integration" mean?

options.a: It refers to American immigrants in Europe.
options.b: The term refers to the halt of immigration to Europe.
options.c: It refers to European emigrants in the USA.
options.d: The term refers to the union of European states into the EU.

en.context: European integration is the process of countries joining together to collaborate on economy, politics, and laws to create a stronger union. Fun fact: The EU started with just six founding countries to ensure peace after World War II!

text: Wer wird bei der Europawahl gewählt?

options.b: die Länder, die in die EU eintreten dürfen

en.text: Who is elected in the European elections?

options.b: the countries that are allowed to join the EU
options.c: the members of the European Parliament

en.context: These elections allow citizens to directly choose who represents their interests in the European Parliament. Did you know these elections take place every five years across all member states?

text: Welches Land ist ein Nachbarland von Deutschland?

en.text: Which country is a neighbor of Germany?
en.context: The Czech Republic shares a border with Germany to the east, making it a close partner in trade and culture. A cool tip: You can travel between these countries without passport checks thanks to the Schengen Agreement!

text: Wo ist ein Sitz des Europäischen Parlaments?

en.text: Where is one of the seats of the European Parliament?
en.context: Strasbourg, located in France, is the official seat of the European Parliament and symbolizes the unity of European nations. It's a beautiful city where lawmakers from all over Europe gather to debate and vote!

text: Der damalige französische Staatspräsident François Mitterrand und der damalige deutsche Bundeskanzler Helmut Kohl gedenken in Verdun gemeinsam der Toten beider Weltkriege. Welches Ziel der Europäischen Union wird bei diesem Treffen deutlich?

options.b: Reisefreiheit in alle Länder der EU
options.c: Frieden und Sicherheit in den Ländern der EU
options.d: einheitliche Feiertage in den Ländern der EU

en.text: The then-French President François Mitterrand and the then-German Chancellor Helmut Kohl jointly commemorate the dead of both World Wars in Verdun. Which goal of the European Union is evident in this meeting?

options.b: Freedom of travel to all EU countries
options.c: Peace and security in the EU countries
options.d: Unified public holidays in the EU countries

en.context: This symbolic meeting showed that former enemies could move past their history to build a future of lasting peace and security. Verdun is now a powerful global symbol of reconciliation and friendship!

text: Wie viele Mitgliedstaaten hat die EU heute?

en.text: How many member states does the EU have today?
en.context: The EU currently consists of 27 member countries that work together to ensure peace and prosperity. A helpful tip: the UK was the most recent member to leave, an event known as "Brexit"!

text: 2007 wurde das 50-jährige Jubiläum der "Römischen Verträge" gefeiert. Was war der Inhalt der Verträge?

en.text: In 2007, the 50th anniversary of the "Treaties of Rome" was celebrated. What was the content of these treaties?

options.b: The establishment of the European Economic Community (EEC)
options.d: The establishment of the Oder-Neisse line as the eastern border

en.context: The Treaties of Rome established the European Economic Community (EEC) to create a common market. This was a brilliant move to ensure lasting peace by linking the economies of European nations!

text: An welchen Orten arbeitet das Europäische Parlament?

en.text: In which locations does the European Parliament work?
en.context: The European Parliament operates across these three cities to represent the diversity of the EU. Interestingly, while Strasbourg is the official seat, Brussels is where most of the daily political work happens!

text: Durch welche Verträge schloss sich die Bundesrepublik Deutschland mit anderen Staaten zur Europäischen Wirtschaftsgemeinschaft zusammen?

en.text: Through which treaties did the Federal Republic of Germany join other states to form the European Economic Community?
en.context: Germany joined the European Economic Community through the Treaties of Rome in 1957. Germany was proud to be one of the six founding members that started this journey toward European unity!

text: Seit wann bezahlt man in Deutschland mit dem Euro in bar?

en.text: Since when has the Euro been used in cash in Germany?
en.context: Germany introduced Euro banknotes and coins in 2002, replacing the old Deutsche Mark. It's a great tool for travel, as you can use the same money in most EU countries!

text: Frau Seger bekommt ein Kind. Was muss sie tun, um Elterngeld zu erhalten?

options.a: Sie muss an ihre Krankenkasse schreiben.
options.b: Sie muss einen Antrag bei der Elterngeldstelle stellen.
options.c: Sie muss nichts tun, denn sie bekommt automatisch Elterngeld.
options.d: Sie muss das Arbeitsamt um Erlaubnis bitten.

en.text: Mrs. Seger is having a child. What must she do to receive parental allowance?

options.a: She must write to her health insurance provider.
options.b: She must submit an application to the parental allowance office.
options.c: She does not need to do anything, as she receives parental allowance automatically.
options.d: She must ask the employment agency for permission.

en.context: To receive parental allowance (Elterngeld), you must actively apply through the official office as it is not granted automatically. It's a wonderful support system designed to help new parents balance their finances and family time!

text: Wer entscheidet, ob ein Kind in Deutschland in den Kindergarten geht?

en.text: Who decides whether a child in Germany goes to kindergarten?
en.context: In Germany, the decision about when a child enters kindergarten rests with the parents or legal guardians. A helpful tip: you'll often hear these centers called 'Kita,' which combines daycare and kindergarten!

text: Maik und Sybille wollen mit Freunden an ihrem deutschen Wohnort eine Demonstration auf der Straße abhalten. Was müssen sie vorher tun?

options.b: Sie müssen nichts tun. Man darf in Deutschland jederzeit überall demonstrieren.
options.c: Sie können gar nichts tun, denn Demonstrationen sind in Deutschland grundsätzlich verboten.
options.d: Maik und Sybille müssen einen neuen Verein gründen, weil nur Vereine demonstrieren dürfen.

en.text: Maik and Sybille want to hold a street demonstration with friends in their place of residence in Germany. What must they do beforehand?

options.b: They do not need to do anything. In Germany, you are allowed to demonstrate anywhere at any time.
options.c: They cannot do anything, as demonstrations are generally prohibited in Germany.
options.d: Maik and Sybille must found a new association, because only associations are allowed to demonstrate.

en.context: While freedom of assembly is a fundamental right, you must register a demonstration in advance so authorities can ensure the safety of all participants. This is part of Germany's vibrant democratic tradition of public expression!

text: Welchen Schulabschluss braucht man normalerweise, um an einer Universität in Deutschland ein Studium zu beginnen?

en.text: Which school-leaving qualification is normally required to start studying at a university in Germany?
en.context: The Abitur is the general higher education entrance qualification required for university study. It's the rewarding culmination of several years of specialized schooling in the Gymnasium track!

text: Wer darf in Deutschland nicht als Paar zusammenleben?

en.text: Who is not allowed to live together as a couple in Germany?

options.a: Hans (20 years old) and Marie (19 years old)
options.b: Tom (20 years old) and Klaus (45 years old)
options.c: Sofie (35 years old) and Lisa (40 years old)
options.d: Anne (13 years old) and Tim (25 years old)

en.context: German law strictly protects minors; romantic relationships or living together are prohibited if one person is under 14 years old. This ensures that children are protected from exploitation as they grow into adulthood.

text: Ab welchem Alter ist man in Deutschland volljährig?

en.text: At what age does a person reach the age of majority in Germany?
en.context: In Germany, you are legally an adult at 18, meaning you can vote and sign contracts independently. Fun fact: You can actually start learning to drive at 17 with a companion!

text: Eine Frau ist schwanger. Sie ist kurz vor und nach der Geburt ihres Kindes vom Gesetz besonders beschützt. Wie heißt dieser Schutz?

en.text: A woman is pregnant. By law, she is specially protected shortly before and after the birth of her child. What is this protection called?
en.context: Maternity protection ensures that mothers can focus on their health and their newborn without risking their employment. It is a wonderful support system designed to protect both the mother and the baby during a critical time!

text: Die Erziehung der Kinder ist in Deutschland vor allem Aufgabe …

en.text: In Germany, the upbringing of children is primarily the responsibility of...
en.context: German law recognizes that parents have the primary right and duty to decide how their children are raised. While parents lead, the state provides helpful resources through the Jugendamt to support families!

text: Wer ist in Deutschland hauptsächlich verantwortlich für die Kindererziehung?

en.text: Who is primarily responsible for the upbringing of children in Germany?
en.context: Parents are centrally responsible for their children's development and values. A key part of this responsibility is ensuring that children attend school, as education is compulsory in Germany!

text: In Deutschland hat man die besten Chancen auf einen gut bezahlten Arbeitsplatz, wenn man …

en.text: In Germany, one has the best chances of getting a well-paid job if one...

options.d: is a member of a political party.

en.context: Strong educational and vocational qualifications are the key to career success and higher wages in the German labor market. Germany is world-famous for its 'Dual Education System,' which blends classroom learning with practical work experience!

text: Wenn man in Deutschland ein Kind schlägt, …

options.b: geht das nur die Familie etwas an.
options.c: kann man dafür nicht bestraft werden.

en.text: If you hit a child in Germany, ...

options.b: it is only the family's business.
options.c: you cannot be punished for it.
options.d: you can be punished for it.

en.context: Physical punishment of children is strictly forbidden by law to ensure their safety and well-being. Remember, every child in Germany has a legal right to be free from violence!

text: In Deutschland …

options.a: darf man zur gleichen Zeit nur mit einer Partnerin/einem Partner verheiratet sein.
options.b: kann man mehrere Ehepartnerinnen/Ehepartner gleichzeitig haben.
options.c: darf man nicht wieder heiraten, wenn man einmal verheiratet war.
options.d: darf eine Frau nicht wieder heiraten, wenn ihr Mann gestorben ist.

en.text: In Germany, ...

options.a: you may only be married to one partner at a time.
options.b: you can have several spouses at the same time.
options.c: you are not allowed to marry again if you have been married once.
options.d: a woman is not allowed to remarry if her husband has died.

en.context: German law only recognizes monogamous marriages, meaning you can only be married to one person at a time. On a positive note, Germany welcomes love in all its forms and recognizes same-sex marriages!

text: Wo müssen Sie sich anmelden, wenn Sie in Deutschland umziehen?

en.text: Where must you register if you move in Germany?
en.context: Registering your address at the Einwohnermeldeamt is a legal requirement that helps you access health insurance and other essential services. It's the official way of saying 'I live here now!'

text: In Deutschland dürfen Ehepaare sich scheiden lassen. Meistens müssen sie dazu das "Trennungsjahr" einhalten. Was bedeutet das?

options.b: Die Ehegatten sind ein Jahr verheiratet, dann ist die Scheidung möglich.
options.c: Das Besuchsrecht für die Kinder gilt ein Jahr.
options.d: Die Ehegatten führen mindestens ein Jahr getrennt ihr eigenes Leben. Danach ist die Scheidung möglich.

en.text: In Germany, married couples may get divorced. In most cases, they must observe the "separation year." What does this mean?

options.a: The divorce process takes one year.
options.b: The spouses are married for one year, then divorce is possible.
options.c: Visitation rights for the children apply for one year.
options.d: The spouses live separate lives for at least one year. After that, divorce is possible.

en.context: The 'separation year' is intended to give couples a chance to reflect on their relationship before making a final decision to divorce. It is a period meant to encourage reconciliation or a peaceful parting.

text: Bei Erziehungsproblemen können Eltern in Deutschland Hilfe erhalten vom …

en.text: When facing parenting problems, parents in Germany can receive help from the ...
en.context: The youth welfare office (Jugendamt) is there to support families with counseling, childcare, and guidance. Many parents find their support services incredibly helpful for creating a happy home life!

text: Ein Ehepaar möchte in Deutschland ein Restaurant eröffnen. Was braucht es dazu unbedingt?

options.d: eine Gaststättenerlaubnis von der zuständigen Behörde

en.text: A married couple wants to open a restaurant in Germany. What is absolutely required for this?

options.c: approval from the residents' registration office
options.d: a restaurant license from the competent authority

en.context: To ensure health and safety standards are met, the government requires a specific restaurant license before you can serve guests. Germany is famous for its high quality and safety standards in gastronomy!

text: Eine erwachsene Frau möchte in Deutschland das Abitur nachholen. Das kann sie an …

en.text: An adult woman wants to complete her Abitur (school-leaving certificate) in Germany. She can do this at ...
en.context: Adult secondary schools provide a wonderful second chance for adults to earn their Abitur and qualify for university. In Germany, lifelong learning is highly valued—it is never too late to pursue your dreams!

text: Was darf das Jugendamt in Deutschland?

options.a: Es entscheidet, welche Schule das Kind besucht.
options.b: Es kann ein Kind, das geschlagen wird oder hungern muss, aus der Familie nehmen.
options.c: Es bezahlt das Kindergeld an die Eltern.
options.d: Es kontrolliert, ob das Kind einen Kindergarten besucht.

en.text: What is the youth welfare office (Jugendamt) authorized to do in Germany?

options.a: It decides which school the child attends.
options.b: It can remove a child from the family if the child is being beaten or is starving.
options.c: It pays child benefit to the parents.
options.d: It monitors whether the child attends kindergarten.

en.context: The youth welfare office (Jugendamt) exists primarily to protect children and ensure their safety and wellbeing. The legal concept of 'Kindeswohl' (child welfare) is a top priority in German society.

text: Das Berufsinformationszentrum BIZ bei der Bundesagentur für Arbeit in Deutschland hilft bei der …

en.text: The Vocational Information Centre (BIZ) of the Federal Employment Agency in Germany helps with ...
en.context: The BIZ offers free, professional guidance to help you find a career path that fits your talents. Germany's 'Dual Education System' is world-renowned for blending classroom theory with practical work experience!

text: In Deutschland hat ein Kind in der Schule …

en.text: In Germany, a child in school ...

options.a: has a right to unlimited free time.
options.b: has freedom of choice for all subjects.

en.context: School attendance is mandatory (Schulpflicht) to ensure that every child, regardless of background, has equal access to education. Education is seen as the most important foundation for a successful future in Germany!

text: Ein Mann möchte mit 30 Jahren in Deutschland sein Abitur nachholen. Wo kann er das tun? An …

en.text: A 30-year-old man in Germany wants to obtain his Abitur (high school diploma). Where can he do this? At an...
en.context: Evening schools (Abendgymnasien) provide a great opportunity for adults to earn their Abitur while continuing to work. It's never too late to learn—lifelong education is highly encouraged in Germany!

text: Was bedeutet in Deutschland der Grundsatz der Gleichbehandlung?

options.a: Niemand darf z.B. wegen einer Behinderung benachteiligt werden.
options.b: Man darf andere Personen benachteiligen, wenn ausreichende persönliche Gründe hierfür vorliegen.
options.c: Niemand darf gegen Personen klagen, wenn sie benachteiligt wurden.
options.d: Es ist für alle Gesetz, benachteiligten Gruppen jährlich Geld zu spenden.

en.text: What does the principle of equal treatment mean in Germany?
